suited more for 6+ and older. a few things here les enfants is french for childrenit doesnt refer to infants or toddlers. for those who dont know, a quick note newborn birth to 4 weeksinfants 4 weeks to 1 yeartoddler 1-3preschooler 3-5child 6-13that said it does state not to spray it on a babys skin. applying gently on clothes is the preferred method. though to be honest, id say this is more for ages 6+while baby cologne isnt something i would use on a baby, it could be better for adults who want a light scent and not overwhelmed by a perfume.for almost $30, this is an 8.5 fl oz spray. compared to some perfumes and colognes, this is moderately priced.smell? it isnt the greatest. i do like it as a light, after-bathing scent. it seems more suited to a room freshener than a perfume. but, it isnt the worst perfume/scent ive smelled.the generous size means it will last a while.would i get it again? no. nor would i recommend it either.

not sure if this is a joke. i honestly dont even know where to begin with this review i have, in my lifetime and, wearing fake nails for over 25 years never seen this type of idea ill be as short as possible with this most anyone who hasnt had an amputation, usually has 8 fingers and 2 thumbs total of 10 digits and, in most cases the nails are usually shaped the same.thus, in a set of nails youd need: 2 thumb nails 2 index fingernails 2 middle fingernails 2 ring fingernails 2 pinkie fingernailsi know sounds almost silly or stupid to mention, but thats math. total nails needed: 10 (luxury 12pcs press on nails kit comes with 10 different sizes which is pre-numbered 0-9 in a box choose the most suitable size that fits you every finger). hard to fit every finger when the nails are different sizes.and, were not all sized the same. hence why some packaging is no less than 24 nails with 12 sizes. i know because ive bought nails like this for years! just look up kiss and youll see what i am talking about.this?was this a joke? this is a set of twelve (12) nails 10 different sizes! unlike the model hands i dont wear my nails over the edges of my finger. that is not how you wear these nails to begin with and definitely not with nail glue. even being fake, the nails should at least attempt to look realistic. see how the salons do them, look on google (or your favorite search engine).who thought this was a great idea? and, these have to be the largest sizes for the particular nails ive seen. these really are a fancier version of the lee and wet n wild from the late 1980s/early 1990.whats worse? even the packaging states: 24did they not think anyone would notice that detail?ill admit, these are pretty. and, thinking it was a half package, i contacted amazon for a return/replacement. i cancelled the replacement, but i am returning these. as i told the rep theyre pretty useless as they are. there is no way i can use these. i would, at minimum, only get 4 nails worth of coverage. for this price? nope. not at all. and, i feel sorry for those who do have to pay.in addition, the nearly $16 price tag (excluding sales tax) for 12 nails? unreasonable. in my case, id need two (2) sets which would run $32. for that price i might as well just wear a mask, go to the salon, and have them do it for me. my nails would definitely last two weeks, and i wouldnt have to do them myself.i will not be getting these again and do not recommend them. the seller might have a better set out there, but not this selection.

underwhelming cleanser. this is an exfoliating facial cleanser that really didn't feel that great or give me that much in benefits. for $23 it is not something i'd get again or recommend. it was slightly overpriced and overhyped.

beautiful but need to be cautious when using. this is, for less than $10, a lovely comb.can function at a wedding or other event. however, the cons outweigh the beauty.given how delicate this is, there is no storage box or bag. so, after use where does one put it? that is an issue for me. even a simple velvet type bag would be a good idea.second? the comb itself.while absolutely stunning in design function is a bit questionable because of the construction. the combs are open design thus loose hairs can weave in, particularly those with thick and curly hair. this makes removal or readjusting a bit difficult if not a tad uncomfortable.this is also for thicker hair, not thin, and thus circles back to the issue i just presented.while pretty, it isnt something i am particularly pleased with. it isnt something i would get for myself again. i also wouldnt give it as a gift.

great for travel. as most have said here these are great for travel, especially when you cant find soap around but have water. though i wouldnt carry a bar of soap, i have used travel size body wash as a hand soap. if it cleans the body, should clean the hands, right?anyway the metal container i got was dented, but that is okay as i dont intend to travel where i wont have soap. so, im using these at my bathroom sink.let me state this as a warning: hands must be dry before grabbing a soap sheet.i know all caps is yelling, but trust me it is for a very good reason. as one reviewer said, even a drop of water can damage these.basically i grab a one with dry hands, hold it in palm, turn water on, let it dissolve. good lather, i dont really smell a scent, hands wash clean. they dont dry out, but i do use a moisturizing sanitizer, and hand lotion after.i wouldnt say these go years if youre like me and using them as an alternative to other hand soaps to preserve waste, they might last about a month (8 washes per day, 30 days). if only as travel backup they might last a few years. again, it depends on how theyre used.i personally find them a bit on the pricey side at almost $10 before tax. thats just me.i do have other soaps, and these should last me a while. i would not recommend them as a body wash. as opposed to carrying a bar of soap, try a travel container with body wash. just my suggestion.

perfect manicure set. colorful manicure and pedicure kit for home, gym, or travel.has most everything needed and easy to store. love the pretty case. price is great as well.each tool is functional and works great.

not the worst, but definitely not the best. i really wanted to love this scrub there was just too much wrong with it to even like it.first of all this is far too thick to squeeze out of the tube. i had to smash it with my arm to get it to move.the scent? if youre looking for bath & body works type of cherry blossom, youre not going to get it.once you get it out, and can take the scent after it is applied, it is a soft, moisturizing and no slip scrub.this isnt something id get again or recommend.

gold standard of lamps. this uv/led lamp is by far one of the best ive used. i have another one by ovlux (one of my favorites). so, when i saw this, i didnt hesitate to get it to have on standby.there are a few issues with it, but im overall pleased.very easy to use.

nail clips. i mainly got these for a home-made gel soak off (using foil).and, they worked! they dont pinch at all; even my mom used them and said she could barely feel them.light weight and fairly durable. there are enough for two people to use at the same time, or extras on hand if they ever break.if used for poly gels, im sure they would work just as well.

not so starry. thank you in advance to the amazon vine program for providing an item to review. a positive review was not required, nor was it requested, and all words are my own.getting my nails done is not just a relaxing experience, but it is also an expressive/creative one.seeing this colorful and glittery set i had to get it.i dont know about others experiences mine did not come with a brush or applicator for the little gel pots. i remembered what my nail techs used to use small make-up brushes and thankfully i had some. that seemed to work.the black polish is necessary to get the desired effect. i chose the purple glitter gel and after a few awkward moments i got a pretty decent manicure. more of the black showed through, there were moments when the purple glitter reflected, but it wasnt what i thought.i havent tried the other colors. but, this is fairly decent and easy to use. not my favorite as there are other brands out there that are even easier to use.overall, im not too unhappy with it. its not something i would likely purchase again.

bigger than expected and super cute. ***i received this item for free on the condition i provide an honest review****first, this bag is big! but not too big. if you see the photos, it's bigger than the 16.9 ounce water bottle. it has a easy to use zipper with no rough spots. the embellishments are nicely done and affixed snuggly. the material is soft but sturdy and the shade of pink is fabulous.this would be ideal for travel or everyday in a larger tote.great price point!

hydrates and protects. this is a gentle and hydrating mask. even though it has a retinol alternative, you can use it over a stronger serum. this is almost like a sleep mask, not all of it absorbs, it keeps a protective layer on your face while you sleep. it really hydrates so you look rested even if you arent.

hard to squeeze out. i love the smell of this and is is very hydrating. the only issue is that it is so thick that it is hard to squeeze it out from the bottle. you need 2 strong hands to get it out. this is definitely annoying.

fabulous. this is great eye cream, it hydrates and erases fine lines. it even gives a bit of a glow and looks great under makeup. the cream is thick and rich, one of the best ive ever used. and it will last a long time!

great mask. thin bamboo sheet sap infused sheet facee masks with rice essence extract cream for intensive moisturizing for pore reduction for all skin types, product made in koreathe power of rice, for your skin care : with an innovative delivery system, this korean rice cream topping facial mask sheet actively works to deliver the skin enhancing power of our proprietary hydrating rice extract cream faster and deeper to restore suppleness and moisture.this is a pretty neat idea for a mask. the mask is soaked in serum, you put this on and then you add the rice cream on top so it soaks into your skin after the serum does. its quite hydrating and soothing.

works quite well. ive never tried anything like this product before. you put it on, wait 5 minutes and then wash your face with it. over time it really does make my skin look clearer and brighter. i dont use this daily, just a couple times a week. it could be used daily though but it is kind of expensive.

gentle but effective. this isnt the strongest vitamin c cream ive used but it is better than most serums. i seem to see better results with lotions like this. im not sure if the lemon adds anything, the aloe might be why i can use this daily without irritation. it is gentle enough for day use though you might want to use sunscreen with it.

love this, so hydrating!!. i love this mask. it is very hydrating and the mask is thicker than most and clings very well. the benefits last much longer than others i have used. this is by far one of my favorite masks and itsa good deal for the price.

so hydrating. i love this mask almost as much as an avocado sheet mask i have. it really hydrates your skin and it is so soothing. ill be buying more of these for certain. i used this after a chemical peel that i use and it really took care of the usual redness i get.
hydrating. this is one of the better ha products that ive used. its great for keeping my skin hydrated all day and hiding fine lines. can be used at night and in the morning under make up.

what a relief!. i dont know about you but i have been having dry hands from lots of hand sanitizer. and my poor cuticles. this hand cream is very rich and hydrating without leaving any greasy residue or taking forever to absorb. this is excellent quality hand cream and my hands are getting better. i only need to use it once or twice a day.

works well, a bit too tingly. i just recently started using the paper charcoal bubble masks but i do love peel off masks even more. i put on a medium layer of this and after a few minutes it starts to bubble. unlike with the paper masks it was almost too tingly that made me want to just wash it off. but it went away after a few more minutes and then i just had to wait for it to dry. maybe 20 minutes total. i like to leave peel off masks on longer to tighten my pores. it came of in just a couple pieces which was unusual, usually you have all these scraps left on. my skin looked and felt very fresh after.

hydrating and light. this is a very hydrating gel-cream moisturizer. its light and absorbs well. it lasts all day, i think it is perfect for morning use under makeup. i like the green color too. its almost as good as moisture surge, better than the similar gel cream by neutrogena. id definitely use this on an ongoing basis. highly recommend!

very small but nice. this is a small oil diffuser than others i have seen or used but it is very nice and looks better in person than in pictures. the light design is nice but too much for me when im sleeping. you have to turn off the whole thing to turn the lights off. you can turn on the diffuser without lights but once on i can only get them off by turning off the diffuser. it lasts almost through the night maybe 6 hours? i find taking off the ceramic cover easier than covers of other diffusers. i use this for just my bedroom so it is perfect. i did not come with a measuring cup for water, this is unusual but i had an extra so no big deal.

waste of money. i'm disappointed. mostly in myself as i should have read the ingredient list carefully. this is just a glycerin soap with chemicals and a negligent amount of honey and lots of heavy perfume. it's a bit drying for my face. i will probably finish this as a hand soap.i'm going to continue to look for a better, more natural honey soap.

absobs quickly, not greasy. i have always loved this oil but i haven't used it in years, i thought it had been discontinued. i was given a bottle of the lightly scented version to review. i can be sensitive to some scented products, they can give me headaches. this one is light enough to not bother me at all. because the weather has been so warm, i add peppermint oil when i use this. it is refreshing and cooling. i am a big fan of using oils to moisturize my skin, though than can be greasy. what i like about this oil is that it absorbs quickly so i can get dressed right away if need be. i usually just towel off to slightly damp skin then use this oil and air dry. it really makes my skin soft and smooth. i'm glad this oil is still around. when i run out, i will definitely buy more.
great for the price!!. the perfect addition to your day time routine. n3 no name can serve as a perfect and easy to apply primer. the scent is light and pleasant. the finish is fresh and smooth.

gentle effectie cleanser, made in korea. this jelly facial cleanser from vitamasques is made in korea.the tube has a foil safety seal under the white cap. the cleanser is brown with a jelly-like consistency. it does have a scent, a mix of green tea and tea tree oil. i do notice the scent when using the cleanser but the scent doesnt linger.a small drop is enough to wash my face and neck. it doesnt make a lot of lather but does feel silky going on the skin. i really like the consistency of it.and i really like how well the cleanser works! it cleaned two layers of sunscreen grime and sweat off my face. some face washes dont remove all the sunscreen residue, and i have to keep washing my face or use an exfoliating scrub. this cleanser? one wash and done. i was amazed by how well it worked.im in my 30s, white, with sensitive combination skin. the cleanser did not irritate my face at all.

cute and functional. if i have a choice between a plain headband and a unicorn headband, well, im picking the unicorn headband every time!these headbands are super cute and colorful. the faux fur is soft and doesnt shed, and really adds to the unicorn aspect. the horns stand up from the headband, and are sparkly silver. the ears dont stand up as well, theyre a bit floppy, and also have sparkly silver on the backs.the headbands are on the smaller side. i measured approximately 6 inches across the opening with a headband unstretched, and about 9 inches when fully stretched. i like that the headbands are tight, they stay on my head and hold my hair back. i dont have to keep fussing with them, they stay in place. they are about 2 inches wide, which helps with comfort.

one side works, but that's all i need. i like one side of this silicone back scrubber from lnlofen. the soft brush side, the one with the longer bristles, works great for scrubbing my back. i can actually feel the bristles move against my skin, and so i feel like im getting clean.the other side, the stiff brush side, just slid right over my skin. i didnt feel the bumps at all.im 55 and slender, the back scrubber is long enough for me.the package includes a hook to hang the brush in the shower, but i just loop it over the shower rail and it dries quickly. the brush can roll up into a compact bundle for storage.

great files. this pack of 16 nail files from keiby citom is a great value.i started doing dip nails at home, and the worst part is getting off the old manicure. i have to file off the layers of top coat, which can take a while if i dont have a good file. i prefer a glass file for actually shaping my nails, but theyre not good for removing layers of polish.using one of these files, i had the top coat filed off all my nails in less than ten minutes. the 100 grit side is perfect. i actually asked a nail tech friend what i should use, and she told me 100 grit.the files come in a zipper seal plastic bag that can be used to store them.the edges of the files arent sharp. im talking about the edges that touch your skin; sometimes these can be sharp, especially on pro files, and need to be seasoned to prevent cuts. no problems with that here.

looks cool and works well. the look of the lassie hair dryer is what caught my eye first. it just looks cool! i have the pinkgray color. the gray is a medium shade with a smooth finish that wont pick up hairs and dust. the pink is a metallic magenta that really pops.the speed controls are on the handle. the slide button doesnt move easily, so theres not a big chance of accidentally changing the speed/temperature without meaning to. on the back of the handle is the option for either cool or hot temperature.the hair dryer doesnt dry my hair any faster than any other hair dryer ive used. but i like that i can set the temperature to cool to lessen damage and not have to hold in a cool shot button.the hair dryer isnt as lightweight as some plastic models ive used, but its weighted pretty well. the weight of it is distributed evenly on both sides of the handle.the attachments have little tabs that line up with notches on the nozzle, and then you twist them to lock them in place. i like it -- theres less chance of them popping off while i use the hair dryer.the bottom of the hair dryer says the rated power is 1800 watt, which is contrary to the description which says it is 2000 watt.

very strong. i tried the cedarwood essential oil from sweetvally. the oil is so strong that i smelled it even before removing it from the box or breaking the seal on the bottle. the bottle is tiny but mighty, i guess!the oil almost pours out from the bottle, so be careful when adding this to a diffuser or anything else. i accidentally added too much to my diffuser the first time i tried it, because the oil dripped out so quickly.the cedarwood scent is very woodsy. i personally find the scent calming. i read it can also help keep insects away.the bottle says the extraction method is steam distilled, but does not say where the oil was distilled or anything regarding the country of origin.

aloe vera gel. the description for this aloe vera gel by cimiy skin care confuses me a little. one of the uses suggested by the manufacturer is to put the gel on before going outside, which can protect skin from sunburns. this gel has hyaluronic acid listed as an ingredient, which can actually make skin even more sensitive to uv rays.aloe vera is great for soothing sunburns *after* exposure. this gel absorbed into my skin after about a minute, without leaving behind a sticky or tacky feeling. ive used some aloe vera gels that have left me a sticky mess, but not this one. so i like the quick absorption.the gel does have a slight scent to it. its a fresh-smelling scent that sticks around for a while.this aloe vera gel would also be great for homemade hand sanitizer.this gel is manufactured in china.

workman's friend ultimate hand care bundle. for me, i like the hand cleaner and barrier cream the most in this bundle. i found that the superior healing hand cream was just average. these products might seem dude oriented, but they work for anyone.superior hand cleaner with activated charcoal (individually 4/5 stars):--works great to remove grease and odors. i like the pumice in the cleanser, as it gives me a real clean, scrubby feeling.superior barrier skin cream (individually 4/5 stars):--actually does help prevent grease or oil from staining my hands. also helps keep my fingernails from becoming a dirt mess when i weed without gloves. but doesnt provide much of a moisturizing effect.superior healing hand cream (individually 3/5 stars):--truly unscented hand lotion. but its just average as a lotion -- it took a few days of 2-3x per day applications to help heal my cracked hands.

pharmacopia tangerine-flavored lip balm. i spend 99% of my life with chapped lips, so im always looking for a chapstick or lip balm that will actually work.i was intrigued by the hemp oil in the pharmacopia tangerine-flavored lip balm. but not all of the ingredients are listed in the product information. i am allergic to beeswax, which is the second ingredient in the lip balm. so i was unable to use it.im including a picture of the tube, but for reference, here are the ingredients: helianthus annuus (sunflower) oil, beeswax/cire dabeille, olea europaea (extra virgin olive) oil, aroma (flavor), cannabis sativa (hemp) seed oil, rosmarinus officinalis (rosemary) leaf extract, tocopherol (vitamin e), aloe barbadensis.

cordless water flosser. if youve never used a water flosser before, theres a definite learning curve. expect to get wet the first few times, if not more. the learning curve and mess is worth it -- my mouth generally feels cleaner after using one -- but i dont love this particular model.i wanted a water flosser that wouldnt take up a lot of space on my bathroom counter. this one is a sort of all-in-one, with the reservoir built into the handle, but i kept getting water bubbles in the tank/tip, which made flossing take a lot longer.theres only 3 speeds: low, medium, and high. the low is quite low. and because of where the speed switch button is, its really easy to accidentally switch speeds while using the flosser.the flosser charges via usb, but with a proprietary end (not a or c or anything like that).
overly rose scented. overly rose scented and it upset my scalp. left it red and flaky.
great addition to your skincare routine. soaks in well, didn't irritate my skin. helps perk my skin up in the morning. well packaged, pump works great.

smells wonderful. smells great and didn't upset kiddos skin like some of the stronger scented lotions we have do. a bit pricey for body lotion and i'm dreading trying to get the bottom of the product out, otherwise it is quality stuff.

left my face shiny and greasy. this didn't absorb well at all. it sat on the surface of my skin forming a shiny barrier nothing else could get through. i tried it a few times using less and less, thinking that i was just putting too much on. nothing helped. i had better luck using it overnight as a deep hydrator. but it didn't play nice with my face cream, and prevented it from soaking in. in the morning my skin felt heavy and it was oily enough that my hair ended up greasy in the front from touching my forehead while i slept.

overpriced, smelled odd. it is a pretty small bottle for $25 body lotion. also, a little does not go a long way with this one and i had to use more than i typically do. overall, i would say it was decently moisturizing but not amazingly so and certainly not enough to merit the price.as for the smell, it was just odd, like slightly organic but not strong. just off-putting. faded quickly though.

happily surprised. i was a bit skeptical before trying because i find most hair masks to be more hype than anything else. this one however was great for my long thick wavy mess of a head of hair. it left my hair soft, manageable, and even a bit shinier than before. my hair felt hydrated without being weighted down, and wasn't left straw like which has happened to me in the past when i try to deep condition. i can usually go about 3-4 days between shampoos and didn't find myself needing to wash my hair any sooner than that. obviously, if you shampoo more often on the regular you will have a different result than me. but basically it didn't leave my hair feeling dirty the next day.my process: i shampooed and then like most masks and conditioners i kept it away from my roots and only applied it from the midshaft to my ends (otherwise i end up with oily roots). then i waited... and waited... and waited. since i was reviewing the product i wanted to make sure i waited the full 10 mins to give it a chance. i was out of stuff to wash and shave after about 3 min so the last 7 were pretty boring. in the future i will probably only wait 5 min. i didn't apply any other conditioner other than my normal heat protector i spritz onto towel dried hair. blow-dried as usual and the results let me skip the flat iron.i was pretty please. i find this an easy mask to recommend. i have spent much more and gotten less of a result. i also like that it didn't leave my hair smelling perfumy afterwards.
helped my heels. this one seemed a bit gentler than the others i've tried in the past. if you have sensitive skin, then this would be a good alternative. it was still effective just not as "intense".

huge but work really well. when i first got these i was like what kinda 80s looking crazy huge thing did i get. then i put one in my thick med/long hair and was impressed. no pulling, hold well and didn't slip out. plus, the size means they really do fit comfortably around your wrist.highly recommend.

shampoo is stellar, rest is okay. shampoo is great, the conditioner is okay, and the vitamins didn't really do anything. hubby doesn't really need a great conditioner though since his hair is pretty short so we weren't to concerned about that. i tried the conditioner just to see and wasn't a fan. i have frizzy tangle prone hair and this just wasn't moisturizing enough. but i also usually buy conditioner specific to my hair type so i can't fault this one too much for not being it.

pleasantly surprised. i ended up really liking these. i was a teen in the 90s and loved my scrunchies, so happy to see them making a comeback. these worked well in my thick hair, although the satin ones do loosen up a bit but only because the material is slippery. you can wear all day without getting that annoying hair pulled headache feeling.i think my favorite are the middle ones, that have a small tail, they look super cute. they and the long ones are more of a scarf material. i've liked the whole set. great for the price too.

worked as intended, smelled nice. kiddo liked this, but probably because of the kid friendly scent. he isn't prone to burning so i had to try it as well, and it kept my very fair skin from getting red even after i got drenched from the hose repeatedly. i prefer a lotion feel than the gel, but he didn't mind the consistency. all and all it did what it was suppose to.

of the three i have tried this was the worst. i tried a couple other charcoal peel off masks before this that were much much easier to use, both a bit more expensive but neither crazy so. luckily i had no issues with the bottle splitting like some others reported. here is what i did not like about the product.issue #1: this one didn't peel off in nice big patches more like you had to chip away at it which was messy time consuming and harsher on my face than it needed to be.issue#2: it also was ineffective, it didn't unclog anything. at most it removed a top layer of dead skin making my skin feel softer (and dryer), but after rechecking my pores with a magnifying mirror (if you want to hate yourself buy one and then freak out at your skin, lol) there was no real difference. the before and after of my pores under the mirror were nearly identical.so all that work and annoyed skin for nothing. mostly though i was just aggravated that it came apart in a million little shreds rather than actually just peel off my skin. that's the best part, booooo.

plenty of room for thick and long hair. this thing was great and worked as well as my diva darling microfiber hair towel. i'm not sure i'd call any of the microfiber towels rapid drying but they do pull out more moisture and create less frizz than a standard bath towel. i have super thick wavy (coughfrizzycough) hair that is just past my shoulders, and i had plenty of room to spare with this towel. it could easily work for even longer thicker hair.it wrapped so easily and really stayed put with the little elastic band in the back. my other is a standard towel so you have to twist and tuck and hope it stays. not with these, super secure. plus, there is 4 of them so i never have to time laundry and shower again.highly recommend.
cheaply made, arrived broken!. this product arrived broken and it is not letting me file a return/exchange because it says no returns!
looks real!. i love the side pieces on these clip in bangs extension. they make it so much easier to blend with your natural hair. i dont have enough hair for bangs so this was the perfect solution.

moisturizing, wish you got more for the money. this stride foot cream comes in a little tub. its quite thick and felt a touch touch greasy going on. it instantly softened my feet and smoothed my dry skin. it absorbs fairly quickly. i just dont think i would pay $27 for such a small tub of it.

did not affect my sensitive skin!. after extensive patch testing i discovered that this eye corrector serum works great on my sensitive skin! i love the pump. it goes on smooth and smells amazing. i havent noticed a difference in my eye puffiness but i think it helps my dark circles and its nice and moisturizing.
worth the price. i love these lashes and have already went in for seconds

great at the price point. it's not the best i've ever used, but at the price point it's good. the main thing is to be careful not to use too much at first as it takes very little. that took some adjusting to. i might stick with this one for a while. it's pretty good.
love!. works well on my tail bone length hair. i use it to comb my hair after a shower when it is wet. great for massaging the scalp too. attractive. sturdy. no complaints or regrets.

giftable. i am familiar with the kopari brand. they are considered a "clean beauty" brand with less toxins than most. this is a fun little set. not overly sticky. imparts a nice shine and makes lips feel good. non drying. these would make a great gift. no complaints or regrets.

lasts all day!. surprisingly like this. "romance" ends up being a more neutral and not an in your face color. i have light to medium cool undertoned skin. goes on shimmery purple, wipes off to a pinky / coraly / red. stays all day. i usually use it under other lipstick so when the lipstick fades there is still color there. i have sensitivities to chemical and toxins and this does not seem to bother me that i immediately see. overall ... no regrets. better than expected.
work well. good sharp scissors. have not seen any rusting on these. feel good in my hands. cut well. good quality hair cutting scissors. no regrets.

lip applicators. [[videoid:5c532f5f5d94b55721fdf6748ddcc641]] i want you to try these for two reasons, first of all because i often make my own diy cosmetics, including lip balms and glosses. these applicators are perfect to use with those.also, have you ever found that perfect shade of lipstick that you can't live without, only to find that it's been discontinued? these applicators help get every last bit of lipstick out of a tube so that you can enjoy it longer.as you can see, the colors are pretty bright. i think that these would look more upscale in more muted colors, and if that's what you're going for, i would suggest getting the other color option in these. to be honest, what i would prefer, is if they were paper or wood instead of plastic. a little more environmentally friendly.still, they're good for what they are. the doe-foot tip is slanted, just like with your favorite tubed liquid lip products. they are fuzzy, so they hold the lip product.makeup artists, as well as their clientele, will appreciate that these can be used once and then tossed. no spreading of germs and bacteria.

great for diy.... not only are these sheets good for do-it-yourself masks, but they're fabulous at making any good serum a high-end mask.what i like to do is open one of the individual packets and pull out the tablet, which is a compressed mask. and then, i take very hot water in order to get it to open up. after cleansing my face, i will load it with serum, and then put the warm mask over my face. it's easy to pat it into your skin to make it stay.lean back and relax, i generally do it for half an hour.remove the mask and gently massage the remaining serum into your face, and continue with your facial routine of moisturizer, sunscreen, whatever you use next.so, whether you dip them in pineapple juice, apple cider vinegar, or whatever your diy routine is, or you use a prepared serum from your favorite brand, these are convenient, easy to use, and relatively inexpensive.i especially like the individual packaging because they stay sanitary. i can pop a couple of them in my makeup bag and always be ready to go on any adventure that requires an overnight stay.thanks for reading.

very light, very pleasant. definitely a clean fragrance that's fine for anyone.to me, the scent is pettigrain, orange blossoms.as you would expect, it doesn't have a lot of longevity. but after application it's quite uplifting. not overpowering.absolutely suitable for babies and toddlers, as well as teens and moms.

vintage glamour in red. from pin-up girls to starlets, from old hollywood movie queen royalty to the girls at the automat, certain red lipsticks have dominated makeup counters for decades.this is one of them.everything about this lipstick, except for the modern wearability, is vintage glamour. the shape of the tube and the shape of the lipstick itself harkens to another era. perfect for making the ultimate cupid's bow pout. it even has that slight fragrance that will remind you of vintage lipsticks.i normally wear red that has a little more blue in it, so was surprised that this clear red was fine for me. to me that says that it will flatter a wide range of skin tones.after swiping it on my wrist and then wiping it away, it left a nice stain. if you aren't interested in that drop dead gorgeous look and want something a little more subtle, it does double duty by leaving a pinky red stain when blotted. but go for the high voltage dramatic red if you want to be noticed.very comfortable lipstick, but i didn't have a problem with it bleeding. non-drying and non-cracking. opaque coverage with one swipe.another plus, it makes your teeth look super white.i hope that this review as well as the photographs can help you to make a decision about this lipstick. it's really hard buying such things online, especially when it comes to luxury brands like this.

great for sensitive skin. i got these because they have aloe vera. i have sensitive skin and these do a nice job of gentle cleaning, low odor with no ill effects. they are also nice and portable as i can put them in my bag when i travel.

conditioning mask. as a big fan of hair masks, this is one of the better ones that i've used.i have fine and fragile hair, that is thinning due to heredity and age. this mask smooths it down and makes it look to be in better condition.even the ends don't look as fly away.if you have hair with issues, this is a good product to try.
wolf cut. after taking this wig out and giving it a good shake, the cut on this is fantastic! it's the super trendy wolf cut.the color is fun, but i have no problem wearing this wig out and about.

couldn't be easier. the built-in brush makes this product easy to apply. if you need to put your makeup on in a hurry, this stuff is really great.you can control how much comes out onto the brush and then use the brush to apply it evenly. it works as well as most primers in that it hides some of the medium, if not the large, pores.easy to tuck in your makeup bag.
cute clips. this review is for the large size. the size is something you might want to consider if you have thin or fine hair. just know what you're getting.they hold well and are super cute.

refreshing lemon. these are wonderful for freshening up face and hands. on the box, it's suggests that you can wipe down surfaces. they aren't antibacterial, and they don't clean, but they will leave a nice refreshing scent. on many surfaces, they will leave streaks, as well as not really cleaning.i suggest using them mainly as a quick way to clean your hands on the go without alcohol or chemicals that you might not want on your skin.

artificial looking. perhaps it's just me, but i found this product to be very artificial looking. nothing natural about it including the color.please keep in mind that it might be just the right color for you, but this didn't make my salt and pepper brows look any better.

does what it's supposed to do. although this is a pretty cheaply made set, it actually does the job reasonably well. not just for puppies. i have a standard poodle that has a curly coat that is cropped for the summer. it works very well on his tight curls.

beautiful color. for some reason, it's hard to find extensions in this color. it seems that anything with a mixed gray isn't very popular, or at least not popular enough to mass produce.that's a shame because it's gorgeous. not only if you have gray hair, but if you want a really interesting contrast color.clip-ins are easy to use if you are a novice.

soft color. this goes on dark, but doesn't deposit enough color for me. if you like just a subtle look, it's fine. i confess that i use it without rinsing it out because otherwise the color isn't enough for me.

good bang for your buck. you can't beat these for adding some volume and thickness. there's a bit of a learning curve but thankfully there are plenty of youtube videos out there to help you with installation, even if you're doing it by yourself. it helps to have two mirrors so that you can see the back of your head.very pretty and a good value.

nourishing mask. this is a place on, pat on mask.you remove the mask and carefully place it on your face and smooth it down, and leave it on for about half an hour. while it's on it feels just delightful. it contains hyaluronic acid on a biocellulose sheet that clings to your face. it's as if you can feel all that good stuff soaking into your skin. also contains ceramides, another skin-loving component.after removal there's no need to rinse. you simply pat any remaining solution into your skin.a great way to pamper yourself as well as nourish your face.

pretty natural looking. although i had to trim the bangs a little bit as they were too long, this is a very pretty wig that has natural gray coloring.the hair itself is soft. other than trimming the bangs, it's ready to wear right out of the package.

you get what you pay for. definitely not for the professional, or for precise work at home, but good enough for little touch-ups.the price reflects the quality, or vice versa, so it still gets four stars. if the price were higher, it would be different.the tip of the cutting scissors is not to a point, but the blunt isn't filed down either. so these are going to need the same amount of care that you don't poke someone's eye out as you would with a needle point scissors. kind of defeats the purpose of them having been blunted.comfortable enough for my average size female hands, and a nice feature is the rubber nub that keeps it from clacking together as you close the scissors.

good enough. decent enough but wish they were sharper, especially at the ends. i use the ends of the snipper to do hang nails and trim the corners of the fingernail itself. granted i use them for more than cuticles, which wears them out faster, but when i think of the name soligen i expect a product of superior quality.

easy extensions. clip-ins are so convenient. and i find that they're one of the easiest types of extensions if you're doing it yourself, without anyone else to help you.for the money, you'd be hard pressed to find better extensions. the hair is very nice, the color is stunning.the clips are nice and sturdy, i expect no problems with them in the future.it's important to remember to wash them carefully. dip in water that has a little mild shampoo, and gently squeeze. carefully lift up and place in fresh water.

pamper your body and soul. highly fragranced set, beautifully hand packed with tissue.you can order this with confidence if you are giving it as a gift, because an outer cardboard box protects the gift box itself from any shipping damage.just opening it up is a delight. the aroma is soothing with natural fragrance. not a hint of off chemical odors, just fresh essential oils, blended for a peaceful experience.i especially like the petite candle that is a lovely way to complete the experience. you can have a candle lit bath using the bath gel, and spray the linens with the pillow spray for a calming and sleep inducing experience.very caring gift for someone facing challenges and stress

nice little gifts. these little tubes of hand cream make wonderful gifts because they are packaged each in a special little box. great stockers or just a little token of appreciation.this review is for the olive variety.i didn't find it to be the most moisturizing hand cream that i've tried but it isn't too bad. considering that it's so giftable, i feel that it's definitely worth the money. you can always try one before you give away the others.very light fragrance suitable for just about anyone.

very gentle. i'm so glad but finally the united states is getting access to some of the top skincare in the world that originates in korea.as the listing states, these are gentle exfoliating pads that peel away and dissolve microscopic dead skin gradually and gently. within a week you can see brighter healthier skin that was hiding under the surface.and it does it all without irritation!as with any acidic facial care product, it's important to remember sunscreen if you go out.

antimicrobial mask. i found this mask to be quite comfortable as well as easy to breathe through. suitable for any gender, and it's even a little stylish with an on-trend color as well as a very helpful strap. now when i choose to take it off, i can have it hanging around my neck, ready to pull it back up if necessary. the ear loops are adjustable with a little bead that by now most of us are familiar with using.it claims to be antimicrobial because of some copper content. it's also quick drying as well as offering uv protection.made in korea.

sleep in a bottle. this line uses the well-researched benefits of olive in order to create a skin care that rejuvenates and heals. this particular product is great for morning puffy eyes. i keep it in a cosmetics cooler in the bathroom so that it's colder than room temperature when i pat it on.makes you look bright eyed and ready for the day, even if you had a rough night.

but is it right for you???. it really is like a mild botox treatment!ummm, i may or may not know that from personal experience, haha!okay, yes, i know it from personal experience.as a little background, paracress has been long used as an anti-inflammatory and numbing herb, it's even called the toothache herb.it also 'numbs', as in relaxes, the skin.guess what, that's exactly how botox works.while it isn't going to have nearly the same dramatic effect, i found it to be enough of the difference to where this is always going to be in my makeup/skincare bag.clinical trials using it in this way have indicated that it works on all but about 25% of the participants, so there's a chance it might not work for you. i'm thrilled to be in the percent that it did.it has some of the same chemical components as szechuan pepper, which has got to be safer than the botulism in botox, right?paracress is almost unheard of outside of brazil. this will be our little secret!for reference, i'm in my early 60s. i've always taken care of my skin and stayed out of the sun, so i probably have fewer wrinkles than most people my age, but trust me, i've got them.hopefully this review will help you to make a decision about whether or not you'd like to make the rather substantial investments in this product. it's definitely a risk since it doesn't work for everyone. and i can't stress enough that it's not going to be super dramatic change. but heck, i'm excited at even small changes.xxoo

good for mature skin. although i'm not thrilled that this didn't have a safety seal on it, the cream itself is very good. it's a heavy cream, despite the company identifying it as being light and whipped. it looks whipped in the jar, but it is not light on the face. i don't think it would be suitable for all ages. because of the heaviness and intense moisturizing, i believe that mature skin is going to benefit most from using this.for reference, i'm in my early 60s, in a dry climate, with combination skin (t zone) and some wrinkles as a point of concern.this cream works very well with all of the above. i don't put it on my nose, of course, but when i feel like a splurge i put it on my hands because it's so moisturizing.the claim that it moisturizes 24/7 is indeed true. even in this dry area, my face feels hydrated all day when i use this cream.it contains milk protein, yogurt extract, blueberry extract, rice protein, and a host of other skin-loving ingredients.i hope this review has helped you to make a decision about whether or not you'd like to invest in this product. it is indeed generously sized at 3.4 oz, and it has a very high quality ingredients. but like i said, it's not going to be for everyone.xxoo

like a peach bellini for your face!. do you like champagne cocktails? this one smells like a combination of peach and champagne, and even looks like it!it comes out like a gel. it's activated by water, so after washing your face, leave your face damp and put a thin layer of the gel quickly all over your face. include your neck if you like.then with each passing moment, it's going to get foamier and foamier. that's why you have to have a very thin coat. you only need a small amount of product. if you use too much, your face is going to foam up like you have shaving cream all over!after about 5 or 10 minutes, you massage a little bit with warm water and then rinse. after you dry your face it's going to feel squeaky clean. if you have dry skin, you're ready for a moisturizing serum and cream. if you have oily or acne prone skin, use the appropriate products for that skin type. i don't feel that this mask is drying, but it does leave you very clean.so, in my opinion, this is good for all skin types, as long as you use it as a brightening mask as opposed to a hydrating mask.this is just what your skin needs if you have overdosed on hot humid weather.please look at the ingredients to see if you have any sort of allergy or if any of them are a trigger for any skin issues. because i have sensitive skin that can be triggered, but i only left it on for about 4 minutes. i had absolutely no irritation, but i think if i had left it on longer, i might have. that doesn't mean it's bad for you. if you leave a mild castile soap, which is so gentle, on your face for any length of time, skin will be irritated.made in the usa, from tonica skin in marblehead massachusetts.i hope this review has helped you to make a decision about whether or not you'd like to try this fun mask. it really does leave your skin feeling and looking energized, but it's a bit of an investment. nice splurge for a spa night, and because the product comes out via a pump, it's going to be sanitary for all the participants.it's going to make for some really fun and instagram worthy photos!xxoops, my 'spa night' pal doesn't look too impressed

why this is a good value.... this is pure genius.after unscrewing the top of the cap, you'll see the film that protects the batteries. after you remove it, put the top back on and separate the light unit from the jar.the red led light will be activated by pressing against your skin. does that light actually do anything, or is it a gimmick?there are a number of high-priced red light units on the market. they were first used in dermatologist offices and med spas. when they first came out, i had a red tanda unit. this is exactly the same concept, and yes, you will have a slight difference.the amount of cream gone in the jar has been for about 10 applications. i do it at night so i can relax while holding the unit at various places on my face. the red light goes on when you press slightly on the metal ring, as you can see in the photographs. as you press it to the area of your face that you want treated, slide it from the middle of your face outward, for 3 minutes. then go to the next area. this way you get a little bit of a lymphatic massage. if you suffer with sinus headaches, this action is very good at offering some relief. the cream is very lightly fragranced with a fresh scent. it's a gel or mousse type of cream.after purchasing this, you can get the cream refill.so much cheaper than buying an led light device like a tanda.

too expensive? get the refill pack!. i'm a sucker for beautiful packaging.here we have a gorgeous tea canister that is well made and beautiful. it also closes tightly, keeping my tea fresh. a good tea canister is not cheap, so i didn't have sticker shock with this product.thankfully, the company thoughtfully puts out refill packs of this delicious and calming tea.i'll definitely be ordering a refill because not only do i like it myself, but after going through so many different calming and sleep inducing teas, this is my spouse's absolute favorite. not just because of the taste, but it helps her to totally relax and even sleep throughout the night. other teas and sleep formulations such as melatonin and valerian supplements just don't do the trick.for me, it's more about the delightful taste. slightly floral, slightly sweet. yes, it does relax me and is helpful as part of a nighttime ritual, but not as much as my spouse. we both love it but for different reasons.and i just adore the look of the canister. a lovely gloss ceramic, with an art deco nod, that fits in any decor. no hiding it, it's proudly on the counter, ready for anytime you need a little tea comfort.

similar masks, but all are delightful. this brand of mask is not off the chart expensive, but it's always helpful to try things out before making the big investment.what a terrific way to try the various masks that this company offers! the quad pack includes skin resurrection for rejuvenating, chillax for calming, magic mushroom for moisturizing, and glow and go for brightening.perfect for a pajama parties with a spa theme!the ingredients are similar in all of them. they all contain skin-loving aloe vera as well as cucumber extract, and even manuka honey. and all of them are pleasantly scented and very refreshing to use.

calms stressed, sensitive skin. when skin is stressed, every little thing bothers it. too much heat, too much cold, too much wind, too much dry air.those of us with sensitive skin know exactly what stressed out skin is. we need something that will soothe, without any irritation.this particular cream hit that goal for me. i used it during a rosacea flare up, to help the redness and discomfort calm down. it worked very well. not a miracle, but it worked well.if you have skin like mine, i hope it works for you.

natural plant oils mixed with avocado oil. i wanted this oil because it's a blend that contains both lavender and patchouli. i absolutely adore patchouli, although some products that contain it don't have that wonderful incense sent.this blend is delightful. you get the patchouli and spicy lavender mixed with a little bit of citrus as well as yang yang. there is even a touch of nutmeg to add even more warm spice.i also love the elegant dropper, so much nicer than a rubber dropper. the gold top has a button that depresses to dispense a drop at a time.a very special oil. you don't need a carrier because it's already in skin-loving avocado oil.it has a shrink wrap to protect against contamination, as well as to assure that you've gotten a brand new product. very important to me.oils can go rancid quickly, and this manufacturer puts an expiration date on the bottom. any product that can go rancid should have that, but not all do, so that's another great thing about this oil.

calming blend. anything that helps during these stressful times, even a little, is important.this has a delightfully soothing scent. perfect blend for a moment of calm.nice little gift to send to a loved one that is feeling stressed or anxious.

terrific value. most wigs in this price range look, well, wiggy.when i'm wearing a colorful or super fun hairstyle in a wig, and people can assume rightly that it's a wig, i have no problem with that. i love wearing wigs that look like wigs.but once in awhile, you just want to blend in with the crowd, and wear a wig that simply looks like you've got great hair.wigs like that generally cost a heck of a lot more than this one. was really surprised to see how natural this one looks. in a lot of situations, and by that i mean if it isn't windy or super bright sunshine, no one would think this is a wig. that makes it perfect for shopping, work, and most situations.i find this style to be nearly universally flattering on everyone. it doesn't matter if you have a chubby face or a thin face, this style is classic because most people can wear it.another thing to like is that this wig doesn't get tangled as long as you keep it on a wig stand.very good value for a very cute wig.

damaged or do it yourself? is it cruelty free..... feathers can be a little controversial. rest assured that these are cruelty free! i used to have a peacock as a pet. he shed those beautiful tail feathers every year, and even though he is long gone i still have some of his gorgeous plumes. peacock feathers are not harvested from the animal, you simply pick them up as they are naturally shed.this set arrived in a plastic bag. when i pulled the pieces out, there were three peacock feather pieces and one headband. each feather piece has a rhinestone accent at the bottom. one is all rhinestone, one is a pearl surrounded by rhinestones, and one is a london blue stone surrounded by rhinestones.of the two larger pieces, one has an alligator clip hot glued onto the felt backing, and one has a strip of grossgrain ribbon glued to the back. although i'm not sure, i suspect that it was at one time glued to the headband, or they leave it to the consumer to glue it to the headband. i tried to push the end of the headband through the middle of the ribbon, thinking that perhaps it could slide on, but it is glued completely, you can't slide it onto the headband.the smaller piece also has an alligator type clip of the same size as the larger clipped piece. they stay beautifully in your hair and are actually comfortable.i wouldn't call this a glamor or fashion accessory set, it's definitely a costume set that would be great for a roaring twenties party. each one would also be a fun addition to a hat. i wear a lot of hats and love to adorn them with scarves and feathers.the following is not part of my review but it is a kind of funny peacock story. naturally, when guests came over they wanted to see him with his tail spread out in beautiful peacock glory. they only open up like that when they're trying to impress someone, and he certainly was never impressed by any of my guests. at the time i also had a miniature pig. that peacock was so in love with the pig that whenever she came near him he would spread out his feathers and shake them hoping she would notice and return his love. of course that never happened, the pig could care less. but whenever we wanted to show him off to our friends, we would walk the pig over to him and he would immediately open his tail feathers with a great flourish! my friends and i were all impressed. maggie the pig was not.

more provacative than most gourmands. my experience with middle eastern perfume has been that they are usually heavily reliant on woods, resins, and incense notes. very sensual, very provacative.this offering blends in florals and juicy ripe fruits in the dry down.that sounds like it wouldn't work, but it does.the fresh musk gives way to a sexy, feminine aroma.if i were to create a magazine ad for this perfume, it would be a woman with tousled hair, still between the soft bedsheets, satisfied smile on her face. like i said, provacative.

fragrance on the go. i love the concept of an individual towelette, not only for convenience, but because i can go from fragrance free to smelling delicious in a heartbeat. lunch with someone sensitive to perfume and dinner with someone who isn't.that being said, i believe that this delivery system lends itself better to lighter fragrances.on unwrapping the packet, you smell a dark, bitter chocolatey, red wine kind of note. definitely unique. usually gourmand fragrances are geared for youth, but this is all grown up and adult.too intense for warm weather, ok for fall and winter.

something doesn't have to be expensive in order to be effective. i'm in my early 60s, with slight rosacea under my eyes and a couple of red spidery veins around my nose. my spouse is on blood thinners, and bruises easily.the small amount i show on my hand is the amount i need for my rosacea and around my nose. not very much at all. it did not happen overnight but it did calm the rosacea down, and while they have not all disappeared, the tiniest of the tiny red lines are gone. gone!this cream has also helped those pesky bruises disappear more quickly.the product contains all of the wonderful herbal and botanical ingredients well-known for healing skin, such as arnica montana flower, green tea, and chamomile extracts.the flip cap opens easily, just in case you have any hand difficulties and are curious about that. you need to unscrew the flip cap and remove the safety seal, then screw the cap back on. i can't say this enough, i love a product with a safety seal!i even like the packaging. various plants and flowers on a celadon background.

pore care dupe. it's hereditary. just like my mother, my nose is peppered with large pores. after makeup application, foundation sinks into them making them even more obvious at times.these products work similarly to that very well-known brand that started the whole anti-pore primer craze.they fill in the blanks, so to speak, so that makeup goes on more smoothly.it works well on my nose, and even worked decently on the one pore i nicknamed 'the pit'.cute packaging that once again is similar to that popular brand.

eco friendly. these are more environmentally friendly option for facial care. they can be used for cleansing or makeup application.packaged nicely in an eco-friendly box, so would make a suitable gift. at first i wondered why they included the bag, but of course, no one wants loose sponges tumbling around your washing machine. simply put them in the bag and when you're ready to do laundry, toss the whole kitten caboodle in the washer. i let them air dry.

nicer than the drugstore ones. these are so much nicer than the ones in the store.this company really has the size and softness of the towelettes correct.a little bit larger, and they cleanse better, as opposed to simply coating your hands with an antibacterial solution.no irritation or dryness.the first second or so there is a slight stickiness, but when it dries, no stickiness whatsoever.

at home or on the go. i often use the olay water activated cleansing cloths. the problem is you can only use them at home. they are not individually wrapped. you have to take one and put it in a ziplock.these are these individually wrapped, and create the same amount of sudsiness.they are pretty close to being without any scent, but i did detect a faint fragrance reminiscent of cornflower, which does not smell like corn, nor does it smell like a flower. slightly citrus.they contain willow bark extract, known for soothing inflammation.no stickiness after rinsing. skin feels clean without being dried out.definitely keeping one in my handbag for any freshening up emergency.

beginner lashes. i love eyelashes. actually, i love anything girly.these would be terrific for someone that is experimenting with lashes for the first time.the band is slightly thicker than most lashes, and you get quite a few for a low price.some of the included lashes had some damage. it looks like they were curled under too high of a heat, and they fizzled a bit. they wouldn't be good enough for going out, but they would be perfect for practice putting on lashes. application can be daunting for a beginner. expect to ruin a few pair the first time you try false eyelashes. use the damaged ones first just to get and idea of how to put them on, and toss them.disclosure: product received at a discount in order to test and review. i have no affiliation with the manufacturer other than agreeing to test their item. all views and opinions are my own.

hefty nozzle. we have been using this nozzle for a few weeks, and there are no leaks.that's amusing because we aren't always as careful as we should be when we are finished spraying.it has hit the cement sidewalk more than a few times, without any damage at all.the feature that allows it to stay on without pressing the lever is quite handy when we top off the pool or are doing a lot of spraying. it saves your hands from getting sore. multiple settings for water sprays.nice quality, has a nice heavy feel in your hand.product received to test with the option to review. no financial compensation is ever accepted, and vendor may request that item be returned. views and opinions are my own.

blades of glory. when i was a little girl, i remember seeing a callus razor of my mothers, and wondered why anyone would use one. now that i am past the age that she was at that time, i confess i found myself on the hunt for the perfect pedicure items for those of us prone to alligator feet! forget the ped-egg, it stays sharp for about a minute. real women use blades! caution, go lightly the first time, and always on well soaked tootsies. less is more, a bit of callus is supposed to on our feet. turn the double edged blade around for the second foot, then toss it, never use more than once. buying them like this is the cheapest way to go. these are great quality blades at a great price.

color my world. [[videoid:5f071d40944ad845a102d811a0949121]] this is an absolutely amazing value in terms of quantity.lots of amazing colors that will delight teens and queens and the occasional club kid. plenty of neutral pans of shadow for more conservative types, lots of great pop colors for the drama types.you will need a good primer, like many shadows they disappear quickly without one. some colors are also much different on the skin so don't be afraid to experiment. sensitive eyes might want to do a swatch test.comes with double applicators, and a handy inside mirror.disclosure: product received to test, with the option to review. no financial compensation is ever accepted, and vendor may request that item be returned after testing.views and opinions are my own. i have no affiliation with any manufacturer or distributor other than choosing to review their item.you are probably considering buying this product if you are reading this. hopefully this review was was helpful, but if not, just let me know what's missing and i'll try to help you.

soft skin and hair. disclosure: i received a sample of this item at no cost in order to test, with the option to review if i so desired.as one that makes a lot of my own skin care preparations, argan oil is a key ingredient in many of them.mixed with rosehip seed oil and a little emu oil, it makes an excellent lotion for chapped and cracked hands and feet.adding some avocado oil makes it amazing as a pre-wash hair conditioner.apply to the hair while it is dry (and scalp if you have a dry scalp), then after anywhere from a minute to an hour, shampoo it out for soft, silky hair.

super soft tootsies. used on it's own or as an ingredient in a diy skin preparation, rosehip seed oil is a multi-use mini-miracle.it can be used on the skin or hair, it even works wonders on dry, ragged cuticles.rough cracked heels and/or scaly feet? i took equal parts of this oil and emu oil, and rubbed it on my feet after dry buffing. socks go on, and like me, you will wake up to soft, silky feet.any oil can go rancid, so keep it in the fridge.very pleased with the quality of this rosehip seed oil i was given to test.

lightweight shower & conditioning caps. these are perfect to use a few times when you do a hot conditioner on your hair. they are not the heavy kind that last, but perfect in certain situations. they are similar to hotel shower caps. easy to pack if you are going to be someone's houseguest.great for deep conditioning your hair. slather on the conditioner, put on the cap, and wrap a hot towel around your head.

spa mask to refresh and brighten. i bought into some hype and bought a set of gold masks. you know the ones...expensive face shaped masks that are somewhere between gummi bear and un-gelled jello. and while using it, you look somewhere between king tut and c-3po, the star wars robot. afterward you feel like a ghostbuster that got slimed.forget all that and relax with nourishing botanicals. the ingredients soothe and help reduce splotchies and redness.spa quality facials at home.
pretty. the little girl loved the brush and the hair ties don't pull her hair out.
colors are bright. color is pretty and the color split looks good.
great stretch. no hair pull so far, and great stretch. worked perfectly for my hair.
easily cleans off makeup. did some deep face painting for halloween and these worked well. has a clean smell to them and didn't dry my face out.
first time eyelasher. nice set for a first timer like myself.
nice shade of brown. nice shade of brown and the hair has a natural thickness to it. using for a cosplay and it matches. value for the money.
smells good. great smelling shampoo, used this morning and it still smells good. doesn't sud up a lot but works good.
my husband approved. my husband is always using my face wash so i wanted to get him his own. he says it made his face feel clean but not dry. the smell was really good too.

fantastic!. this 6-head suttik replaces what seems like an identical motor version with 5-heads. this breed of shavers is the first i've ever used in the steamy hot am shower every day. day after day. what a time saver! it does a fantastic job. it delivers a better shave than my really high-$ screen shaver.6 heads is an improvement over 5. first, it simply covers more area. this is your head ---> a spheroid with protuberances. the 6-head shaver conforms to stuff like your ears and jawline better. 6-heads get the cutter closer to the nooks and crannies where those obnoxious thickets of long hairs might survive for a couple of days before someone points and say, "ewee!"the shaver is small and easy to hold. a charge lasts more than a month. cleaning is easy. i use shaving cream to lubricate, just like a regular razor. a 'dry' shave in the shower might sound like an oxymoron, but you'll know what i mean when you use it once.i'm sold. adds at least 5-minutes of goof-off time in my morning routine. i'll take every minute i can gather. perseverance might payoff with a straight razor shave, but the time saved here to goof off in the am pays off immediately!it's revolutionary.was my review helpful? if so, click "helpful" so i can know.
as advertised. the product delivers. it's a nice hyaluronic serum.value for money. better than expected.

super fancy big bows for little girls. my five-year-old absolutely adores these big, beautiful bows for the hair. the alligator clips are easy for her to use even by herself, and not only does she likes to wear them, but she also loves to put them on her 18 inch american girl style dolls..there is a beautiful array of child pleasing colors and patterns, and these were a big hit at our house.

lovely first essence. this is kind of like a cross between a toner and a serum. i have been using it kind of like klairs moisturizing toner, even though it is a little thinner than that. it slaps on and seems to spread over my face nicely, sinks in very quickly, and provides a nice boost of moisture prior to putting on a thicker serum and then moisturizer.i had been reading about this product for really long time before trying it and it's kind of a cult favorite in the k beauty world. i can see why. it's not flashy, but as a first thing to splash on your face and hold in some of that moisture, it works well and doesn't clash with other products.

invigorating and fragrant. i love the zingy, fragrant scent of the shampoo. it feels relatively unisex, and doesn't leave a lot of fragrance lingering behind after you rinse it out. i followed up with a peppermint conditioner from the natural food store, and really enjoyed the lovely cooling summer pick me up for my hair.the shampoo is not overly stripping, but definitely leans more towards really getting things clean rather than being overly moisturizing. i found that i could use it about three times a week on my long hair, and then needed to do co-washing or something more moisturizing in between. i really like the shampoo and appreciate having something more herbal like this in my regimen.

so much fun!. my kids and i have been doing a lot of crafting which involves placing tiny sequins and gems on to small painted wooden or clay items, and so while we are not using these for the intended purpose of going on nails, we have been going through these quickly using tweezers to get them out and apply to our craft projects.i've been really impressed with the variety of the sequins, the quality level of them with the thickness and color reflectivity being great, and the quantity given. we all really love these beautiful butterflies and how vivid and fun they are.

great when used with the retinol face moisturizer from this brand. i had previously been using lush's mask of magnaminty and had really been enjoying it, however i think i was ready for a little bit more of a "grown-up" facial mask to help reduce the appearance of some of the fine lines i have developed in the year since having my son.i have gotten to use this facemask twice and have been super impressed with the results. i am also using the retinol facial moisturizer as well as an ocean mineral toner and cleanser from this brand, and while i was seeing great results from the other products, this mask really supercharged the results. i look like i have had a full nights sleep, which i can assure you i have not! it plumped up some of my wrinkles and just gave my face that dewy fresh appearance that i remember having in my 20s.i received samples of these products to test, but i can't argue with the results. i will certainly be purchasing more from this line.

a fantastic product at a fantastic price. ive tried other brands of argan oil, and have been surprised that some of the brands almost seem more like canola oil or a neutral oil, to where i almost wondered if some of them are counterfeit. not so with this brand. this brand has the natural scent of the real deal, and is effective to boot. i have a baby, so have been washing my hands about 10 times a day, every time i change his diaper. between that and all of the diaper wipes i use, my hands have been starting to peel and crack, so i knew i needed a solution.this argan oil came with a regular lid, an eyedropper, and a pump dispenser. i used the pump dispenser and set it right next to my faucet so i could get a quick pump of oil every time i wash my hands. well, it only took a couple of days to experience really great results. my hands feel normal again, and i am so relieved. from hard skin that was rough and crackled, to soft, happy hands again.i have also used the product on my split ends, and here, id like to caution you that only a few drops are needed. it is very effective and soaks and well, so you only need a very small amount.overall, this argan oil is one of the best i have found. it comes packaged beautifully and looks like a gift, has multiple options for how you dispense it, and is pure, organic, and has the mild scent of real argan oil, unlike some competitors.
. beautifully packaged. easy to work with. amazing results!! will buy again!

perfect practice head for up-dos, braids, twists & more!. absolutely amazing deal and it came with extra goodies! this yebo set was one of the daily deals and on sale for about $8.50, its unbelievable for the price. i got this thinking my 12 yr old daughter could use it to practice braiding and styling. it came with a head with attached 24 synthetic hair, clamp stand, combs, clips, ponytail holders, bun maker and more. the clamp stand alone is worth $6. the head has a hole in the bottom to attach it to a tri-pod (wig stand) if you have one but if you dont thats ok, just use the included clamp which easily attaches to any table. the hair feels good and for the price its great for hats, glasses, barrettes, earrings, the possibilities are endless. this is more than fine for beginners of any age. i could see younger kids loving this to play with too.

these work fast and are great for traveling!. these mountain falls advanced formula nail polish remover pads work great, they're fast at taking off heavy polish and are perfect for traveling by plane. each single packet containd a thick moist pad that did all ten of my fingers in no time which i appreciate. these do have a smell but it's to be expected with any strong polish remover. there are only 10 packets in the box so as far as price goes, these are costly for regular use but if you have a trip planned or you regularly travel these are perfect.

pure allure by raquel welch in ss 14/88 golden wheat $130. when looking for a wig i always find pictures to be far more helpful than words. hopefully the pictures i've included can help someone make a decision. this wig is less than a week (took a bit of work to get rid of the shine to the point where i felt comfortable). i used dry shampoo and played with the style alot back combing etc..i have been wearing high end long human hair wigs so it was a stretch to try this, but i need to cut down on the money im spending and $130 is much better. it has been awhile since i've ordered a raquel welch 1. because i find "tru life fibers do not last) especially when you add heat 2. her wigs move around on me even with/without wig caps, wig bands, it stays..etc..so i buy combs and clips and sew them in myself just like on a lace wig (its actually much easier on these)..im happy with this for the price, the fact that it was prime and here in 1-2 daysyea!!.. and would order another but it does shed quite a bit which is bothering me considering its 3/4 days old.i like where this wig falls on me (which will be different on everyone). the piece that keeps falling in my face is driving me crazy but i will keep "training" it. pics are taken in in several different lightings. there is an rl 14/88 and ss 14/88.. this is the ss..hope this helps someone..

decent quality and thickness. ive gotten similar ones to these before that barely make it through one day, and slightly better ones that can be reused for two or three days. these are probably the best ones ive had so far, lasting 3 to 5 times, depending on how thick your hair is and how tight you make it. nice multipack, especially if youre on the go, and because theyre small, you can always have a few extra. comes in 4 resealable bags of 250pcs for easy use and storage.

3.5 stars; awful flavor and smell, 4 coats gets a good opaque color. theres such a strong, sweet, artificial vanilla flavor and smell to this that is cloying and sickening. yuck. i hate it.once it sets (about 2-5 minutes, which is longer than most), it looks matte and stays in place very well. it took 4 layers to look fully black on me.would be 4 stars if it didnt taste and smell horrible. 3.5 stars

quite small, but works as expected. i love witch hazel for cleaning my skin and helping prevent breakouts. this works as expected, though the wipes are quite small, and as far as the packaging goes, the hole to take the wipes out is tiny, so its a bit of a pain to get your fingers in there. otherwise good wipes!

nice weight and design, works well. the current price of $17 isnt bad for something sturdy that can last far longer than plastic equivalents. it comes with 10 razor blades, which because they are double sided, can be reused more often, as well as being able to manually sharpen the blades if you really want to go all the way with sustainability. speaking of which, this is a superior choice in those terms, as the blades do not have plastic components and involve minimal packaging when ordering refills. good productsee pic!

rose gold masterpiece!. this ornate crown in rose gold, tones of amber, and hints of ivory and bronze creates an elegant, striking look that will have the wearer feeling like royalty. the size and proportions make it a grand statement piece that matches with a variety of looks, particularly those with warm or earthy colors. great quality, doesnt tangle with hair much, and a bit of weight to make this an absolutely gorgeous accessory.

cleanest tongue ever!. wow, these are so much better than using your brush or those plastic scrapers. my tongue has never felt cleaner; furthermore, the next day, morning breath isnt so bad! your tongue is so clean from the night before, that your whole mouth just seems more fresh the next day. absolutely wonderful! highly recommend.

giftable set, looks as described. im happy to say that these looked the same as the product photos, and they are individually packaged so that you can give each one as a gift to six different people, or keep them all if you want. nice amount of fluid, fairly easy application, though you might need to apply it twice to get the right opacity, and once its dry, it stays put without smudging.
