does nothing. a poor conditioner. false marketing claims.. an unremarkable conditioner. the name-brand conditioners work better and cost much less. this foamiest product does nothing to thicken hair. it does not work well as a conditioner. none of the ingredients are associated with reducing hair loss. the three peptides in this product aresh-oligopeptide-1,sh-oligopeptide-2, andsh-polypeptide-3.there are some peptides associated with hair health, such as ghk-cu and tb-500, but this product does not contain those peptides. to my knowledge, there is no evidence that the ingredients in this product do anything for hair health. i searched online for any scientific studies and could not find anything, or even any theoretical benefits. i could also not find anything in the popular hair loss discussion forums. the manufacturer is making false and misleading claims to push their overpriced product. they are also making false claims about what caffeine does, such as saying that it suppresses dht, which it does not do.

too hard to use. i am surprised this product worked. it does what it claims to do. it gave me a realistic-looking tan and that tan withstood multiple showers and cleaning wipes. the tan looks like i spent the day in the sun, rather than dark brown or orange.the problem with a product like this is the color contrast with the places you do not apply this to, or if you miss a spot, or if you have thinning hair. it can go on blotchy if it is not applied evenly. some parts of the body might become darker or not absorb this as well. making it appear smooth is a challenge. also, where do you stop applying this to your body? wherever you stop there will be a contrast with the untanned skin. one particular problem is your hairline and scalp. i have thinning hair, so the contrast between a pale scalp and a tanned face looks odd. this is a challenging product to use and i wouldn't recommend it unless you are already used to using sunless tanners.it is not greasy, it did not stain, and it dried quickly. the ingredients seem healthy for the skin. there is no smell to the cream.

so far i am pleased with this set, having sensitive skin, it works for me. first, this beard growth kit, 10 in 1 beard kit for men with beard oil, beard balm, beard conditioner set offers a nice number of items if you are considering a gift this holiday season (or any time). its a nice box, again, offers a good number of items for a gift, and is done nicely enough in box set up and presentation it would be well received. second, the items in it are a good range of quality so i think itll be appreciated. what i am saying: the brush, comb, bag, scissors, and guard are decent, but not great quality. you can get better quality, but for working needs and for a gift, this is pretty good. but, for example the scissors feel a bit cheaper. but they will get use as long as they cut well enough. im not big into using a guard, but this one is like others ive had. the included bag is decent, but id never use it; if traveling or moving my stuff id use a toiletry bag. the brush is not as nice as others i have, but its useable and gets the job done. same with the comb. that brings us to the shampoo, conditioner, and oil. note: i have extremely sensitive skin, so this review is based on my experience from that standpoint. i like a range of scents or items to keep my beard in good shape, but i have a far more difficult time finding things that work and are okay on my skin. i have only used these items once, but thats all it takes to know. i come away happy - they all worked and no reaction. i used the shampoo and conditioner in the shower today and they not only had a nice, light scent, but i was bale to let it sit on the beard for a few minutes each, having no issues with skin and liking the smell. the beard felt soft following and i liked how they worked overall. next, the oil. i believe it is claimed to be used for beard growth, and maybe itll help with that. my beard is decent, but i do have spots that could use thickening, so again, i keep an eye open for options. however, there is no way to know if itll work for that for a few weeks if not months. so until then, it again did fine on my skin - not a single ounce of negative reaction. the sect is light, so its nice to smell, but one (my wife) has to get up close to smell it. i do like it that way, so it doesnt bug my sinuses or overwhelm. in the end im very happy so far with this beard growth kit, 10 in 1 beard kit for men with beard oil, beard balm, beard conditioner set and happy i grabbed it. ill use it and if all stays well, would consider getting it again.

so far this is working well for my sensitive skin; will see how it goes in the long run. first off, i have extremely sensitive skin overall, but especially on my face. to that end, i must be very carefully about what i put on. i do tend towards lighter or no scents, but have found some that work alright for me. but with or without scent, i can still have issues. in the end, its how my skin handles anything that is the bottom line for me. in this case, this provence beauty beard oil - all natural fragrance free olive, sunflower & jojoba oils works well for me and so far, im very pleased.i use a derma roller a couple times a week to help with hair in my beard and front of my head. at this point i then add a beard oil to these locations to help stimulate growth. without doing major testing with all the elements, like a control group (area) of my head, its hard to say what works and what doesnt. i feel like the derma roller has a large part in how well growth or at least slowing of loss goes. but, i have also come to believe some of these oils available do play a supportive role. to that point, i wont be able to speak about this provence beauty beard oil - all natural fragrance free olive, sunflower & jojoba oils for awhile. i have only just begun to use it - as a replacement for another oil.but i do like that 1. its not too oily on my hair or skin, 2. has little to no real scent, 3. feels fine and is not a problem on my skin, and 4. that it washes off my hands easily post application. right now i like it as an oil providing at least some softening to my beard. will it aide growth? we shall see...

its a decent idea, it worked, but not with the included suction cups. i drive my wife nuts when i trim my beard in the bathroom - i try to catch all the clippings, but without something like this, it does not work well. i thought id give this a try - it seems like a great idea, but...1. it arrived with 1 plastic bib, 2 suction cups with hooks, and 1 shaving cream brush.2. the plastic bib feels okay - its a thin plastic, but it should last for a decent amount of time. it is not as nice as most of the kinds ive experienced or we own for haircutting3. its stitching is okay - i think itll last decently4. there are two loops on the ends you will attach to the suction cups.5. the suction cups - are worthless. this is why it gets the 4 stars - i would have given it 3 stars if we didnt have our own suction cups to use instead of theirs. one of the two that came with it stuck for 5 - 10 seconds before it stopped working altogether. the other suction cup doesnt even look right - it looks like it never worked and was not made properly.6. we have suction cups (bought on amazon) we were able to use - i looped that part of the bib onto them, which worked, but not designed for this, they would fall off. ill end up taping them to it - at least that way ill be able to use it.7. it fit me fine. it felt fine - just like a haircut cape. the white looks weird - im used to the far more common black - but at the same time i think itll show the beard hair really well.8. i did notice that as i go in closer to the mirror to see what im doing it folds over in front of me and isnt as trapped out - be aware of this because its easy to start missing the catch in the bib.overall its a decent thing to have and itll make my wife happy not getting the beard clippings all over the place. this could be a little nicer - a little thicker plastic, much better suction cups - which would easily earn it a 5 star rating. but for what it is, im happy to have it. if it lasts, itll be great.

not bad. this is the second set of different colors of this brand i have purchased and they are decent nail polish. the colors go on strong and go good with nail art. there is a bit of a funky odor but it's nail polish so you would expect an odor. it did not take long to dry and the colors are great.

beautiful. this kissio 9 eye-shadows pack is really nice! but the fun part is watching the shadow holder spin in circles in your palm. once you open it, the spinning stops. so fun! there's 9 gorgeeeeeeeous sparkly colors to wear. i'll probably wear the majority of them over time. they're really nice and super easy to apply! now that i'm retired, i don't wear makeup everyday so this pack will last me a long time. the shadows look nice and stay on for quite awhile before melting in the 90+ degree weather that were experiencing which created a drought here. but these eye shadows will definitely brighten up your day! i like them!

a very versatile mist spray bottle. these scoot just spray bottles are fabulous for many uses. most people use them for spraying beauty products, plants, hair spray, etc. however, i ordered them for my artwork to spray watercolor paint, inks, and acrylics. what i love about them is the fine mist and that you can see what product it contains. also, once filled, you can turn the lock so there's no leaking. a very versatile plastic water spray bottle!

not very sparkly. the rhinestones in these "flowers" don't catch the light very well, so only one really looked sparkly in the light. each clip has a bottom, 3.75" long clip (with about 3" of that for holding hair) with three 1" long clips (with 0.75" of that for holding hair) attached to it's top. the springs are strong, so they'll hold hair well. however, they're so strong that i have difficultly working them on my own hair. the "flowers" are also felt or some kind of stiff cloth glued to the plastic clip, so the "flowers" may come off with use.

works to dry drippy hair faster after a shower. the package contained 3 microfiber towel wrap turbans: one pink, one lavender, and one striped (orange, pink, blue, white). they are 26" in length. after a shower, i've wanted to wrap my long hair up in a towel for a while because otherwise it drips down my back as it dries. but i've never mastered getting a towel to stay in place. this product does help: place the large "hood" section in place on your head, tuck your hair into the tail then twist the tail one or two times, and secure the elastic loop over the button. it stays in place okay as long as you don't try to pull a shirt on over it (or similar activity). i put it on for about 20 minutes after my shower, and the wrap is pretty wet when i take it off. my hair's still damp, but it dries much faster after that and doesn't drip down my back. so it works.

a winner!. if you're looking for wooden paddle brush for long, fine hair, then this one may be just what you need.this brush is well constructed. it's made entirely of bamboo (the handle, body & bristles). however, the bristles are smooth. this results in a very sturdy brush that can easily work through tough tangles, but without causing unnecessary breakage.and the price is right (under $8.00 with prime at the time of this review).if there's any downside then it would probably not the best brush to use on wet hair with a blow dryer because it's not designed for air flow.however, if you're looking for a good, sturdy paddle brush to use on dry hair, then this is the one for you.

not really sure who this brush is for. while this is a nice looking & seemingly well designed brush, i'm not really sure who it's best suited for.my wife has been in search of a brush for fine hair. and while this brush is described to work on fine hair, it barely even brushed through the surface of her hair.the bristles are so soft that it can't really grip. so just kind of glides over.it may be useful more as a beard brush, but unfortunately, that's not what it's being sold as.giving it 2 stars for being a well made brush (wood handle is very nice), but just not very useful for its intended purpose, brushing!

i have brunerr hair (with gray n it) but my eyebrows are a different thing. i'm just missing, thick eyebrows - i do have hair, but you an see through them...not enough hair. this kit helps me feel as if younger than my 65 years! pick an eyebrow "style," use the frame and included pencil and i have eyebrows again!

my first time using a detangling brush. i have curly hair - very, very fine, but curly hair. because my hair is so fine, i cannot use traditional combs or brushes when my hair is either wet or dry. i mean, all i have to do is one "flip" or have the wind move my hair once and the knots are there. for many years, after shampooing my hair, i used a very wide pick to comb through my hair. even with a pick i have to go slowly since i will find at least one knot in my hair. anyway, this is my first time using a detangling brush and it's not a bad deal. i still have to go slow, but i like how it goes through my hair more fully than the pick. definitely worth a try if you have curly hair!

i had originally thought i might give this as a gift. but i had to open it and spray some on my hand...and that was it. i love the deep, spicy-sweet scent of this perfume. it was a few hours before bed when i sprayed this and when i went to bed, i had my arm up near my nose - oh, it smelled so good and helped me relax and fall asleep! okay, so maybe that's not what you think of when looking for a great new perfume to wear, but it's not that it made me sleepy - it's just that it smelled so good i relaxed quickly and that allowed me to fall asleep. this would have been a great gift for one of my daughters in law, but no...i can't give it away! if you like that deep, spicy-sweet scent of certain perfumes, try this one. so worth it!

i have tried the drills that have batteries in the body of the drill. and while they don't seem to be bad, they just don't have the power that this one does. i'm thinking it has to do with the size/strength of the battery in the drill's attached power supply. while i do use this for my fingernails, i am using it more for my toenails and feet. toenails are thick and tough and i have trouble cutting them well even with good toenail clippers so being able to file them a little at a time is much easier. also, i have some very hard, thick skin on my feet and "grinding" that skin down is much easier that using a foot file. this is a wonderful nail drill that's not just for nails - sturdy like a workhorse, it will make short work of messy nails or hard, callused skin.

i took a chance, but again, i was wrong. see, i have naturally curly hair. the issue is when the weather is very dry over a few days' time, my hair goes flat. curly hair curls a lot in humid weather; hence, the frizz which we deal with! anyway, i saw these and thought that maybe during those really dry winter days these would assist me in getting my hair to curl as it should. now, the problem is, curly hair "fights" with some hair items - from my younger years my sisters used bristle curlers (they had straight hair) but the first time i tried using them, my hair like bit into those bristles and man, i nearly had to cut my hair off to get those demon curlers out of my hair! these are similar, apparently to the bristle, demon curlers as they grabbed a hold of my wet hair and when i tried to pull one off, even holding onto the hair at my scalp, i could feel the tube holding onto my hair for dear life. that was with wet hair...i'm glad i did not wait for my hair to dry before attempting to pull the tube off! i have several friends with longer, straight hair and i'm sure they will have a different, happier and more comfortable experience than i did. what i'm saying is that i'm sure they work - for those with straight hair. if you have curly hair, best be careful when using them!

this is an all-inclusive package for those wanting to get into gel nail polish. i mean, you aren't starting with tons of colors or anything, but when you are new to gel nail polish, keeping it simple is the best way to begin the process. the two colors are nice, but i am sure once a person starts gel polish, they will love it and buy a set of colors that suit them. now, the only thing i would change about this set is the uv light as it is very small. i already had a larger one and am comfortable using my big one as i can fit all four fingers under it with ease. not that i have fat fingers or anything, but i just feel the included uv light is a tad smaller than most women would like. other than that, this kit will get absolutely anyone started properly in the gel nail polishing procedure.

i bought this and another set for my niece to put on me. my niece is a professional and this is my way of getting professional work done without paying what she makes at her shop! of course, she does stuff like this for me and her mom (my sister) every once in a while when we ask! so i got this and another tray for her to put on me and this tray was not really appropriate for my eyelashes - they were just a bit too thin to use on me. the other tray worked much better so next time i will not get this set. i'm hoping she can use it on another client of hers...might work for others, just not for me.

i haven't worn the pearl-covered ones. but i have worn all the rest! i love how these look on my head and they do a great job of keeping my hair back from my face when i'm working around the house. i mean, i've used scrunchies to pull my hair up on my head, but after a little while that hurts my scalp. these are gentle on both my hair and my scalp and they do look good (at least to me) on my head!

sweet orange is amazing!. liquid body washes are my favorites, but some seem to dry my already dry skin out so that right after my shower, i have to slather on lotion to keep my skin from feeling tight and dry. not so with this one! first, it smells so good - love the orange scent that envelopes the shower! second, a small dollop on my scrubby lathers up well and cleans thoroughly without having to add any more to the scrubby (like other body washes). last, when i step out to dry off, my skin feels hydrated and soft. i really like this body wash!

not just for little girls!. i have very, very fine hair - meaning that each strand of my hair is exceptionally thin/fine. i have a lot of hair, but because my hair is so fine, i have trouble with wispy strands coming loose at the wrong places. these type of clip work really well to keep my wispies corralled. granted, these are very brightly colored, but i have a few of the kind that blend into my hair. what i was looking for was something that coordinated with what i was wearing so that i could "celebrate" my hair with the bright colors of these clips. i mean, why hide "difficult" hair issues? or maybe hiding it in plain sight when wearing a christmas-y red dress with red hair clips! if you have wispies that escape and make your up-do look messy, maybe you too could try bright clips to celebrate your wild and crazy hair!

straight razor with a "guard". that's basically what this is. first off, the box is written in chinese, i believe, with absolutely no english on it. no instructions other than a pair of pictures. not great pictures either. not to be deterred, i opened the box, pulled one of the razors from the box, removed the plastic cover and tried it. if you look closely at the one picture, you will see what appears to be a drop of blood - it is. that is why i described this as a straight razor with a guard in quotes. make no mistake, these are sharp. use them delicately and only on smooth skin. if you have a blemish, a mole or anything just slightly raised on your skin, it will be cut off. you will bleed. just want others to be aware of what this is and to take care not to hurt themselves.
great quality. absolutely beautiful craftsmanship, easy to grip with hands, and i love the fact that this is bigger than commercial toothbrushes feels nice !

something different. these bits work well and are compatible with all the nail drills i tried. the bits are good quality and seem like they should last a long time as long as you don't abuse them. the set has some unusual bit profiles that we found very useful, especially for tighter spots or smaller nails. the case keeps everything safe, in one place, and secure.

great set. this is a great nail set. it has all the bits you need to get started. it is well made and spins at a high rpm (12000?) easy to swap out bits and the wand is easy to handle and great for fine detailed work. i only use it on top speed but it does have 3 seed total and can be reversed if needed. the battery holds a charge well and is enough to do a pair of hands. recharge takes about an hour.

nice scent. i'm not usually a fan of flower scented sprays, but my wife loves them so we gave this a try. i was shocked at how well i enjoyed this scent. it was floral, but not the usual sickey sweet scent i usually associate with floral scents. this one was a lot better and almost woodsey smelling. my wife loves it and was thrilled that i liked it too so now out whole house smells like this and she is happy and i can tolerate it! worth a go for sure!

rich shampoo. this is a very rich thick shampoo. salon quality stuff here. amazing lather, great scent and rinses super clean. works amazing for color treated hair, and perfect from stripped hair. the conditioner is also incredible. i used some on my beard and it made it amazingly soft and manageable. i caught heck from my wife as she told me this was to valuable for her hair to waste on my scratchy beard. oh well sometimes you can't win.

nice quality. these shower caps are nice and thick and work very well. they are reusable and fully water proof. we used some of them to cover our metal detector electronics when we detector in the rain. they work perfect for that application as well as a traditional shower cap! we are careful to air dry these completely before storing them away so they won't mold. they are see through so they work well for covers on electronics!

handy. these smaller spray bottles are very handy for sprayer needs. we use them to most our plants, and to spray unruly hair. they are a compact but work very well. nice and thick, but plastic so no worries of shattering them it they get dropped. so far they have worked great and we have not had any issues. the intake hose is long enough to reach the bottom and get most of the liquid. no clogs or extra drips.

granddaughter loves this. my granddaughter (13) is real into nails right now and loves this set. she did her own nails and her friends nails and i will say they did a nice job. she said this was easy to use and worked as well as any other set she has tried. colors were great and the nails were strong and easy to shape perfectly. the only real problem we had was this does have a pretty nasty smell, but we set up a fan and that took care of the fumes. she has added this set to her growing collection of nail stuff, and likes to come over every couple of weeks to open up her "salon" i like it cause i get to spend some extra time with my granddaughter.

nice set. i use tweezers a lot and these are pretty decent ones. they work for holding small screws and reaching hard to reach spots. not super fine tips but work for my needs. i did sharpen and shape the pointed set a bit to use to remove some solder flux in a very tight spot. handy for most any detail work. scissors are so-so and will probably get trashed pretty quick in the shop. the case is super handy and keeps the set together and is easy to slip into a pocket to take with you. these would work pretty good as eyebrow tweezers also.

great for quick use. my wife really likes these for quick clean ups. she uses them for after workout times when she is running late and can't take a shower. leaves her clean and feeling fresh. very gentle on the body even in sensitive areas. has a mild scent and does not irritate at all. wipes is large enough to clean the entire body and is very thick and soft. perfect for travel. perfect for areas that may have little or no access to water.

surprised. i have this a try with a very negative attitude about it. wow i was shocked at how well this cleans ears, and how comfortable (dare i say it actually felt pleasant????) it was to use. it is very soft and gentle on the ears. cleans very well and cleaning the tools was a breeze. case holds everything secure and keeps it clean and ready for the next session. i use this once a week and very pleased with this product. heck even the mirror in the case is super nice and easy to use. this is perfect for all ears.

handy travel bottles. these travel bottles are great for hand sanitizer. super easy to use and very durable. the little holders are strong and attach to any handy ring. bottles are air tight so no worries on leaks. easy to use flip cap allows a quick squirt for a easy to use sanitizing routine. the covers are nice and the fabric is holding up very well. easy clip allows easy removal for taking these bottles where ever you need them.

great masks. these masks work very good. very soothing on the face. my girls have a beauty day about once a month so we are always trying out new masks. these ones work great and they loved how clean and soothing these were. they said their skin felt younger and more elastic. super easy to use and no i'll side effects.

love aloe. i love the way this aloe works to cure dry skin problems and to soothe any skin irritation. i use this on burns, and bites, and any other skin issue. very soothing and long lasting. it cures the issue not just covers it up. super mild that even my sensitive skin is never irritated. smells like aloe and does leave a bit of a tacky- feel (that is what is supposed to do!). this also works incredible in quickly healing blisters and bringing relief to raw skin. great easy to use bottle and no negative issues with this.

good workout bands. my wife likes these a lot for her workouts. it has been a very hot summer and our workouts have gone from a little sweaty to drenched in sweat grossness. these have not only kept her long hair out of her face, by have stopped the sweat from dripping down her face and into her eyes. gross yes but they work wonderful and wash up perfectly. zero complaints.

odd shape. this has an odd shape. it is almost pointed at the front and kinda like a crown. it really is slippery and kept pulling down when used with a mask. without the mask it kept riding up and would not stay put. very large writing on it made it a bit tacky. just didn't work for our needs.

no hair pull. my wife is a big fan of these hair ties. they work well for her longer hair. she generally wears her hair up in some kind of bun and these times work well for keeping everything in place. they stay put but are easy and pain free to remove. just enough stretch to wrap up the hair. these also work well in conjunction with bobbiepins.

a bit large. first this is a real nice pointed top brush. it is well made and very sorry and consistent. the handle is also well made and tight to the brush (no wiggle). brush cleans up and dries soft and pliable. it is a #4 size which is on the larger size for a lot of nail art. this is probably best for solid designs or applying top coats and not for fine detail or complicated designs. my daughter uses it to apply latex masks and peel off base coats.

ok if you want a lot of flex. this is non metal (i.e. plastic). the tips are curved well but are kind of wide (they don't come to a point) and they flex a lot. it takes very little pressure to get them to flex and as far as using this to scrape your teeth... well you would get almost no scraping. this would be great for introducing kids to what the dentist does or for people with super sensitive gums. i did give 4 stars as it it a good tool for removing paint from delicate surfaces and it is non marring. handle is comfortable to hold and i actually use this when clean my air guns as the chambers are small and tight and this won't cause damage and can get into the tight spaces.

awesome combs. i love these wooden combs. they are static free and on a great job of de-tangling the hair. i have issues with static hair and these have solve the problem with the first use. i have shorter hair so i don't even bother with a hairbrush anymore. i really can't evidence which one is my favorite as i tried them all and love them all. i won't go back to a brush or plastic comb ever again as these work amazing, also the finer tooth ones work great on my beard so that's an added bonus!

great for diy beauty masks. my grandaughter and her friend love to give each other facials. these are a huge hit as they are having a ball "building" there own face masks. these are the perfect asorbancy for using as a face mask. we made some simple face oil and water masks and the girls loved them. the researched online and came up with a bunch of recipes that they are going to try on the next beauty day. great effective diy masks. size is perfect and they are durable and comfortable. .
questionable necessity.. didn't work like on tv. returned
very nice. i purchased 3 and the clear bottles trigger gets stuck. long spray time. my white and black bottles are excellent!!!

pretty good. great shaving soap. softer than a hard soap but firmer than a cream. very easy lathering with very good glide, lubrication, cushioning, closeness, and a good post shave effect. initially there was a slight animally fragrance but that soon dissipated after leaving the jar open for a day. essentially a fragrance free product thereafter.

ruined bedding. i purchased for my daughter. she has no problem wearing it. it's soft and comfortable. it stays on all night long. however the black floral one left a nasty stain all over her sheets and pillowcase. i washed it by hand before she wore it too. i hope the stain comes out because these are the best sheets! very disappointing.

it works!. i am impressed as this thing works. gave it to my daughter and forgot about it. after about a month she came to me very happy showing her eyelashes. they are longer and more full. also she tried it on her eyebrows just for fun, but it actually worked on eyebrows too. just wow! nice product.
darker is better. tried the mixed shades with 1b. 1b is longer and smoother. will remove the brown/blonde and just go with dark strands.
too heavy to stay on. it was really pretty, but top heavy so it repeatedly slid down off my daughters head. super bummed!
disappointing.... greasy, low saturation, did not stay in place. i was wooed by the ingredients, but bummed at the performance of the product especially considering the price point.
looks good and material is soft.. looks good, nice and soft. unfortuunately, i got a fluke, there is a hole in it. not worth mailing back, so i will try to repair it. here again, it is betty b.

excellent moisturizer. i received a sample of this calming cream in an ipsy bag and fell in love with it. it feels natural and really has helped the rosacea i have. i've used it in the morning but also at bedtime. it also smells great.
bright gorgeous colors. super bright and vibrant! i love it.
beautiful. good quality, nice looking hair sticks.. cant wait to wear them!

it worked great at first but only a few months after using .... it worked great at first but only a few months after using it, you can literally hear it power down to a slower speed. was just drying my hair with it this morning and it was an audible change in the speed of it's blowing power- and the plug was in ok and buttons were not touched. it would go back and forth from normal to less powerful. buy something else.
very warm colors. i really didnt like the colors. i felt they werent neutral.
so easy and fast to use. this is what my dentist sells in their office. so easy and fast to use! works great... you can totally see results!
amazing scent. i love the smell!!! got it at k mart searched everywhere and found it on here

very natural, doesn't build well. very natural mascara and not as nearly as volumizing as id prefer. it looks nice for running some quick errands on a no-makeup makeup day, but its not really a look that stands out. this can be good for some people who like a natural mascara, but i prefer more volume than this gives me. i don't think i'll reach for this very often. the overall quality seems good, but the formula is lacking for me. it doesn't build very well.

awesome kit of generously-sized minis!. this travel set of products is a complete winner for me, and the sizes are generous for minis. i love the vials of the priming water and the prime essence. i actually prefer these two items more than the tube of primer itself, although it is good too. the two liquid products go on so thin and seamlessly, that its the perfect amount of primer to smooth everything out and help the foundation last. sometimes full-on primers can be a bit too smoothing and it makes me feel like my makeup is going to slide off. everything is very mildly scented (a mix of fresh & floral), but not overwhelming and the scent doesnt stick around. the no pore-blem primer is nice and works well to smooth out specific zones on my face, but i don't use it all the time. for all over coverage, i prefer the priming water, followed by the prime essence, which makes my skin look and feel great, and helps my foundation look awesome.

too small for anything. cant fit any makeup and close. no compacts, no eyelash curlers, no powders, no palettes. if you have super tiny makeup it might fit but then why not just use a pouch? total junk and not worth the hassle to send it back. i donated it.

it's a good wig. it's beautiful hair and it blended seamlessly with mine which i loved! no body knew it wasn't my real hair.the style didn't look great on me and there's a little round scalp on the top which unfortunately showed and looked weird.when i put it on in the morning i kind of felt like i looked like i was in an 80s rock band. that could either be good or bad, i guess. the photo i have here is with the top half clipped back.i probably would have kept it but i found a bug in it which really freaked me out.

works well. so let me start off by saying i have been using ghost bond wig glue for years now. i have always opted for platinum but decided to order the original. unlike following "wig gururs" instructions i follow ghost bonds website instructions and i never have any issues . i have been able to swim with my wigs on and have no slipping it adheres well survives sweating and moisture and can support a 350 density wig. if you properly follow instructions you will have no issues.
nope. kiddo enjoyed it, but it did nothing broke in 2 days. buy a regular toothbrush
cute soaps. gave these out as party favors for my daughter's 3rd birthday party. the kids were all excited about them and my kids use them in the bath every night.
love it!. this makeup covers so well, and the eyeshadow is amazing!

elitepad turns off when using case - but, company replaced. i'm back to edit my original review from about a month ago. i originally purchased the purple case, and this was my comment at the time:"i was really happy with the color and quality of the case, but every time i slide my new hp elitepad into the case, it turns off. if i pull the tablet out an inch, it turns back on. but, it will not stay on if it is fully in the case. disappointing."someone from the selling company contacted me after posting this, and replaced it with a duplicate black case that does not have this same issue and works great. i was really impressed that they went out of their way to resolve the issue. i just figured i was out of my money and the case would go into the trash. i'm upgrading the review from 1 star to 4 stars just because their customer service surprised me (pleasantly) so much!

great size, nice and bright!. this is a really nice lighted, mirrored compact! so many times, the lights aren't bright enough to do you much good, there's only a single regular mirror, and/or the size is just too small to give you a proper look. not so with this compact. you get two mirrors for the price of one, a regular mirror and one with a higher magnification for those times when you need a closer inspection. the light has a lovely, and very helpful, color temperature that's pretty close to daylight, just a bit warmer, and is featured around both mirrors. it's operated by a switch on the bottom, so you aren't wasting your batteries. it runs off of two coin batteries. the mirror is larger than most. i have larger than average hands for a woman, and this compact is bigger than my palm. it's a great size for quick once overs and more detailed makeup application. i even prefer using it over my bathroom mirror for doing my eyebrows.

this little bugger works!. i'll be honest. i was rather skeptical when i received this. it looks sleek and pretty but does it actually work? my answer to that question is a resounding yes. you wouldn't think a little buzzing device could make your skincare products do their best job but boy, oh, boy does it ever. the three settings are for cleansing (also used for extractions), moisturizing, and lifting. each setting causes the device to vibrate at different speeds and achieve different goals. using it is fairly easy. hold the button until you hear it turn on and then give the button a quick press to choose between functions. there are lights above each mode and they will light up when you are on said mode. the included instructions are very easy to follow and explain how to use it in a simple way. it's all about keeping very light pressure, while holding the side bars, which add the effective charge to the device, allowing the ionization process to take place. i have seen a difference in my skin already and i have only used it in each mode 3-4 times. it could be easy to overdo it after seeing the results you do, but the instructions specifically advice you on how often each function should be used so the skin isn't having reverse effects from what you want it to. i think the price for this item is fantastic! i have a reduction in blackheads, my skin seems to glow after use, and my products are sinking into my skin better and faster. this one for the win!

cool once you get the hang of it....but the price.... i'm someone with extremely thick and stick straight hair. my hair loses curl within an hour of me fixing it. i've tried other rotating curling irons and haven't had much success with them, so i didn't have very high expectations for this beach waver. i have been pleasantly surprised. once i got the hang of it, i found it to be pretty easy to use. it features buttons that allow you to chooses which direction you would like to curl each section of hair, and a home button that after each use, and after the previous section of hair is no longer attached, allows the barrel to return to a neutral starting point. i ran into an issue the first few times i used it, which was accidentally hitting the home button while my hair was still around the barrel. this caused the hair to get very tight and the barrel to press up against the scalp. this is very easy to do and i definitely think a design change should take place to correct this issue. the barrel is long enough for my medium-long hair and i can see it needing to be used more than once up and down the length of long hair. the curling iron gets up to 410 degrees max, which works pretty well on my thick hair. i've tried fixing my hair in different ways after using it, to see which method holds the curls longer. i still have an issue with my hair losing the curls a bit to quickly but it's remedied by using styling products before and after curling it. to be honest, i think this beach waver is very overpriced. maybe the results would be better for someone with thinner hair but i don't think i'd pay more than $50 for this curling iron. nice results, but the design flaws make this a product i won't reach for that often.

i'm not sure this is worth the price asked.... i've always wanted to get into the art of doing my own acrylics so when i saw this drill set, i knew i had to review it. firstly, it comes with numerous accessories- a fluffy brush for removing dust from the nail and work area, a set of to separaters for painting, a nail board, and the drill bits themselves. the entire set is nicely packaged and comes nestled in a velvety foam materials. the bits are held in their own clear container within the box. the set comes with a detachable adapter that plugs into the end of the drill and then into the wall outlet. the drill itself is small enough to handle pretty easily, although i can see where it would be difficult for someone with smaller hands than myself (i have large hands.) inserting and removing the bits can be a challenge. the mechanism isn't well made so i have to fight with it. the power cord is much too short and i had to use an extension cord to give myself some wiggle room away from the wall outlet. some of the bits' textures do a better job than the others, while some don't do anything at all. the over all look and feel of the set isn't worth the price, in my opinion. it works a bit too awkwardly to be something i'd use that often.

worth a try!. this kit is a great deal! i've been a devoted user of korean skin care products. for a while, i have preferred, out of several brands and types of sheet mask, the benton bee and snail sheet mask. fantastic product!!!! firms, smooths, soothes. it's great. however, that's not why i purchased this set. i have combo skin that leans towards oily in the heat. super oily t-zone. after doing a ton of research, i decided to give the benton aloe gel moisturizer a try. i'd also heard great things about this foam cleanser. i'm really enjoying the cleanser so far. i wet my hands and face, use only a pea sized amount, rub my hands together to create the foam (not a soapy lather, but a thick and creamy foam), and work over my face. i do this after oil cleansing. this does a great job of picking up any oil residue and dirt, while leaving my face soft and refreshed. never tight and dry, which is the sign of an awful cleanser. the gel moisturizer is okay. just okay. i don't get as oily as i did with lotion moisturizers, which is great. however, i don't have that supple, smooth feel that i'm used to and prefer. i've actually noticed flaking around my nose, which is something i never dealt with after moisturizing. a pea sized amount covers my entire face, it leaves a slightly tacky finish until absorbed ( i always let each of my products absorb for around 90 seconds before applying the next step), and is completely colorless. will i repurchase? no, but i do suggest that you give this a try if your research and needs point you this direction. everyone's skin care needs can vary on several levels. there are universal musts for good skin health, like cleansing daily, moisturizing, and using a good sunscreen. there a a ton a variations of those products, though. finding the right routine and products can be a trial and error process. the main thing is to stick to it. the results are worth it!

beautiful finish and wear time. this is a beautiful foundation. i tried applying this both with a flocked powder puff and a foundation brush. i did prefer the powder puff, which did a better job of picking up the powder through the elasticated net dispenser. the finish is what i would call a "luminous matte" or "lit from within"---it definitely wasn't flat but didn't look dewy or greasy, either. it applied very smoothly over some very textured spots on my skin and covered blemishes well.unfortunately, the shade range sucks. i typically need a shade described as "very fair", which are harder to find in mineral foundations. the 100 light shade is too dark for me unless i also cover my whole neck and chest with it, but the finish is beautiful and i honestly would wear it out on days i'm wear a scarf, for example. i would describe this shade as fair-light or light with a neutral peach undertone.if you are familiar with the tarte loose powder foundation, this seems very similar but without the clay that tends to oxidize. they have the same style of packaging (elasticated net + stopper lid), same luminous matte finish, and similar longevity.

excellent for travel and regular use. these are very sturdy bottles with a smooth pump mechanism. i saw no leaking when i used these to fly with shampoo and conditioner recently.each pump is fairly small and consistent, which makes it very easy to regulate the amount used. i think i will be using these regularly and not just for travel for items without a pump or where the pumps are very inconsistent, like body lotion.

glad honest for men finally got it sorted out and offers two varieties - one exfoliating and one not. gotta take a moment to write a review because this is my favorite soap. the other kind they make (the non-exfoliating kind) went through phases - first it was exfoliating, then they got bad reviews because it was too exfoliating (which i absolutely loved about it), then they removed all of the exfoliation and got bad reviews because there wasn't any exfoliation any more... and finally they got it together to have two varieties, one without any exfoliation, and one with exfoliation - this one. and the other one is now stuck with a 3 star review average just because of that mix-up.at first i was nervous that even with the exfoliation back (this one), it would not be as good as it was before when it had the exfoliation originally, because it used to be oatmeal for the exfoliation, and now it is coffee grounds. i even sent them a note asking why they didn't use oatmeal any more, and they said it was because the oatmeal version would fall apart in the amazon warehouses. but i tried the coffee ground exfoliation - and even though it isn't quite as good - it's still great. this soap beats any other i've tried to purchase, and i have purchased about 10 brands through amazon that all said they were exfoliating. only one of them was exfoliating enough, and i like this one better. (i forget the name of that other brand right now; i'm at my computer. if someone really wants to know, add a comment and i'll dig it up.) i really need a very strong exfoliating soap, and this one actually is strong enough. it also happens to be a brown bar with a decent hearty soap material, as well - not phony - so it is all i need.just wanted to post this review to start to bring back some 5 stars for this company now that they've gotten things sorted out and have two varieties, one exfoliating and one that isn't.

exfoliating version is back!!! now they have two varieties - one with and one without exfoliation. *** update nov 9 2015 ***they have now added the exfoliating version back, in another listing. that means people who love this soap for whatever other reasons without the exfoliation can give it 5 stars for that. people like me, who can't find strong exfoliating soap from any other brand (i have specifically ordered at least 10 other brands, loofah, etc. etc. just trying to find one with strong exfoliation - and none have it, except maybe tasmanian ( (but this is better)), can now use honest for men again - their exfoliating line. so not this listing, but the other listing. not that exfoliating soap should matter, but actually, i'd be scrubbing with rocks from brooks if humans lived out in nature.*** original review a few days before ***this was my favorite soap - and i depended on it for the fantastic exfoliation, better than any other i've tried (and i tried a dozen kinds).all of a sudden, in this last batch, they have simply removed the exfoliation completely. now, when you look at the website, it says "important information: we have recently removed our exfoliating oats and coffee grounds due to customer feedback. our scent is now 100% essential oils no fragrance oil."well, i'm here to give you a 1 star review for this "important information". there are 1,000 other soaps that you can buy with various interesting scents, etc. but this was the only soap you can buy that had great exfoliation.and now they've removed it.hopefully, the 'customer feedback' that caused them to remove the exfoliation will be blasted away by the customer feedback complaining that the exfoliation is missing, and they'll put it back.in the meantime - goodbye, 'honest for men'.

great hair diffuser. i've been trying to get better about regularly going to a salon for a hair cut. for years i just went to great clips once a year. basically, i'd go whenever i hated my hair so i'd have to chop inches off of split ends. but now i have a salon and a hair stylist i love so i'm trying to go every 3 or so months. my stylist recommended that i start using a hair diffuser whenever i dry my hair because i have naturally curly hair.i don't use heat on my hair often but when i do, i've been attaching this to my hair dryer. i have the infiniti pro by conair 1875 watt full size salon performance ac motor styler/hair dryer (that i also bought from amazon) and this fits on it perfectly. i read reviews that sometimes this hair diffuser doesn't work on all hair dryers because the diameter is smaller or larger than what this is compatible. but i measured the diameter of my hair dryer so i knew it would work. and i love how my hair reacts to being diffused. my curls just look marvelous. i almost wish i had time to blow dry my hair every day - almost.i love how it's collapsible so it doesn't take up too much space under my sink. i definitely recommend this product if you're looking for a diffuser.

it works.... just not how it claims too.. after reading the reviews about nail bed damage and how well this helps the soak off i ended up getting it.firstly, it does peel a little nail bed up, so the damage thing is accurate. i wouldn't suggest using it "as advertised". however, after nudging off one nail i dis the standard coarse board, polish remover soaked cotton balls, wrap my fingers in foil thing. the soak off is much easier with this as the base coat. they come off faster, more cleanly and leave behind no damage.i'm only giving it three stars for a few reasons. it doesn't do what it says it should do. it does lift. on my second try, after the first experiment, i did my nails again. several nails lifted and peeled. some came completely free with no damage. usually my gels outlast my level of interest, and i soak them off before i even go the full two weeks. these definitely have a gripping problem. that's not a negative for me. it makes polish changes easier. however, it mean that they don't do what they're supposed to do.bottom line, the things about this product that make it convenient for me are counter to what the product is advertised as doing. i imagine that would make it a poor buy for many a gel lover.

excellent disposable wax treatment. this is an excellent portable paraffin wax treatment. i really love how it makes my hands feel really silky and smooth. i can't really do anything when these are on my hands, but they feel nice and i like relaxing with them. also, the black heart on the outside actually turns red when the wax gets really hot. i used mine in the microwave, and it worked really well.they have a unique design, which is basically a plastic hand wrapper that has a spongy interior, and the sponge holds all of the paraffin wax so that it delivers it right to your hands. it also stays hot for a pretty long time, which surprised me that all the heat didn't dissipate really quickly.these are made for average hands, so if your hands are giant, then these might be hard to fit into. but, they should work pretty well for most hands. also, they're great for the first treatment, but for subsequent treatments, it's really tough to keep the wax in the sleeve while microwaving.but, overall, they're awesome for disposable paraffin treatments.pros:+makes your hands feel silky smooth+the spongy interior absorbs and holds most of the wax+the heat indicator really workscons:-may not last a full five treatments*tgn gave me this wax to review*

works much better than acetone, but watch your surfaces!. do you see 100% natural and think yeah, right! or do you assume it wont work as well as products that have chemicals? i do this all the time especially when it comes to cleaning products or makeup. in this case i was offered a bottle of pure body naturals 100% natural nail polish remover and the skeptical part of me was sure this wouldnt turn out well. but in the spirit of trying new things, i agreed to review it and put it to the test. **see the images for before/after photos**for my test i painted my nails several different colors. we all know deep reds are harder to get off and glitters can also be a pain to remove. i dedicated my left hand to the pure body naturals 100% natural nail polish remover and my right hand i used a drugstore brand of acetone polish remover (the super cheap stuff i usually use.) both hands got two good coats of polish.to remove the polish the pure body naturals remover instructions say to drop or dab it on and wait 2-3 minutes before wiping. for the generic acetone remover i just apply and wipe with a cotton ball.the pure body naturals remover wiped right off, taking most of the polish with it. i did have to reapply a small amount, wait a few more minutes and all but one tiny trace of the red polish was gone. the acetone remover left polish near my cuticles even after a hard scrubbing and a second try.so what did i like?-i like that it works better than the acetone remover, even on the darkest red and glitter!-i like that it did not make my nails feel dry.-i like that the pure body naturals remover doesnt have harsh chemicals.-i like that it isnt tested on animals.-i like that it comes with a handy glass dropper.the only problem (and it could have been a big one) is this polish will eat through any polish, including vanish on my dining room table and styrofoam plates. no kidding. i started out thinking to drip the remover over a plate to keep it off the table but it quickly melted the styrofoam and one small spot of the table varnish.only use this stuff in the bathroom over tile or a sink with no finish/paint/lacquer, etc. never over a delicate surface like your dining room table!i received a free sample of this remover to review. i have given my honest opinion and the real test/comparison results.

new maybuy beard trimmer takes on viking beard -- and wins!. the gene pool i come from has viking, german, and irish heritage -- resulting in a stiff wiry beard that defeats most cordless beard trimmers. to give this trimmer a good chance i followed the directions and charged it for the two hours as recommended in the instructions. my only gripe about this process is that you're supposed to keep an eye on it so that when charging is finished you can disconnect it immediately to avoid potential damage. since the unit has no automatic shut-off when charging is completed, you have to keep an eye on it to avoid a problem. that is my only gripe about this unit. not too many people have the time flexibility to watch it for the two hours (or less) it needs to charge.while it only comes with 3 attachments that is all you really need. the shaver itself is large enough to give you a firm grip and the weight implies quality. the initial effort at trimming my beard was highly successful. i grew it for two weeks to make it a challenge and then shaved it without using any attachments. normally (with other units) i would have expected difficulty in shaving it flush with my skin. typically this type of trimmer is not powerful enough to plow through my beard, pulls some hair out instead of cutting it, and sometimes nicks my skin. none of that happened here. evidently the battery and the motor are up to the task.this is by far the best cordless trimmer i have ever owned and used. highly recommended!

works great for me.. there's something about this conditioner that just works for me. before this, i've never, ever found a conditioner i like. most of them leave my hair heavy; many of them leave my hair feeling a bit stiff or crunchy. often they make my scalp itch as well. funny, my hair/scalp isn't as sensitive to shampoos as it is to conditioners. this shampoo, however, leaves my hair feeling softer/smoother but not weighed down at all. actually, when you're rinsing it out, you can still feel that "squeaky clean" feeling; not that greasy feeling you get with other conditioners. but, it does soften/moisturize the hair. it's soothing to the scalp as well. scent is pleasant but quite subtle.age: upper 50'shair: short; 50% gray; naturalclimate: dry & hotuse of hair product: minimaluse of (heat) styling tools: no

it's pretty good, i guess. i'm not well versed in extensions and the like, but thought this was a great idea to change things up every once in a while. the hair itself is beautiful. no shedding issues, no unusual tangling and no weird smells. i was able to straighten if i wanted to and once washed, it went back to it's beautiful natural texture.my only issue with this is the net is huge and i had issues with how to apply it without it bulking up. as i said, i'm not the best with these things but it seems that this piece could be a bit smaller. my 2nd issue is that the strip of hair to wrap around to secure is very thin. i've seen others that are thicker w/ more hair, which makes it easy to hide where the fake hair is secured to your actual ponytail. my 3rd issue is the velcro kept getting caught on my real hair. you have to be super careful but since it was a bit difficult to secure because of the huge net, sometimes i was going so slow that the hair would shift and i had to start again. kind of hard to explain but i think if i could apply this without worrying about pulling my hair out, it would be easier to secure. anyway, once i got it on it looked great and i'm sure i'll use it here or there. just some things for potential buyers to be aware of.

gorgeous! no one will know its not an expensive tiara.. my daughter bought a 1/2 price tiara at a bridal store for $70, theres very little difference in sizes, quality of the stones and style. this one stands 1/2 an inch taller (better in my opinion for her updo). but the sparkle is there, the silver is realistic, the combs are metal not plastic. its an amazing little crown at a tiny price. simply put... this one will more than suffice for that special day, birthday, prom, wedding, i cant say for how long itll look this beautiful, but for under $10 who cares, its going to last long enough for your special day. especially after you ruin it by getting hair products on it, the expensive one or this one, you wont feel robbed tossing this one.

lashes are a great length, but........ i ordered this set for the lashes since i already have some liner. i am very glad i do have the liner because the one that comes with this set is crap. i made sure my eyes were clean and dry. i read the directions on the back of the package...shake the liner 10 times (i made sure i shook it more than that), apply a thick layer and let dry a few seconds (it took longer than that) apply another thick coat then get the lash and apply it to the liner area. gently press down.very simple right? nope. the edges kept lifting up and the lash fell off after a couple of blinks. ok i thought, maybe my liner wasn't thick enough, so i applied another really good coat and then tried the lash again....same results. i then washed my eye again and tried with my other liner and the lash stayed on like it should.i like the lashes very much. all three pair are a natural length (if you use mascara), and vary in looks. if you get these, make sure you have another liner available or plan to purchase one.
